## PAGE 00873: (2026x Refresh1) Generating Code

- page_id: `303998600`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998600/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+Code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:47.288+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Code engineering
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Demo**

- Code Generation

You can generate code for the selected and prepared set as well as directly for model elements.

##### Code Generation for a Set

Start code generation once the set or sets are prepared. For more details about creating and editing sets, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Code Engineering Sets' space=''].

- Choose **Generate** from the **Code Engineering Sets** item shortcut menu. It allows code generation for all created sets.
- Choose **Generate** from the selected set shortcut menu. It allows code generation only for the selected set.

The **Code Generation Options** dialog appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Generation Options dialog

The **Code Generation Options** dialog allows you to specify the way your code will be generated.

Once you specify generating options for the set, the code can be generated.

| Box Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Output Directory | Type the directory where the generated files will be saved. |
|  | The Set Output Directory dialog appears. Select the directory for the generated files. |
| Set as Working Directory | The output directory is set as a working directory and files are saved to the working directory. |
| Reverse before generation | Changes your model according to changes in the existing code.Exercise caution when selecting the Reverse before generation check box. If the model differs from the code, all differences in the model will be discarded. In these cases, you will lose some of your work. |
| If element deleted from model | To influence the structure of generated code, click one of the following option buttons:Delete code. The representation of deleted entities will be deleted from the code file.Comment code. Deleted entities will be commented in the code file. |
| Use spaces in place of tabs | When selected, spaces (instead of tabs) will be written to the code file. |
| Number of spaces | Specify the number of spaces to be written. |
| OK | The Notification Window appears, displaying how code files are being generated. The Notification Window informs you about problems and errors (mainly file access and syntax errors) found in the code generation process and generation summary. You are also prompted to confirm that you wish to overwrite the file if the output directory already contains one with the same name. |
| Cancel | Closes the dialog without saving changes. |
| Help | Displays Help |

##### Code Generation for a Model Element

All the classes contained in the component will be written into one file. However, code for the class can be generated in a different way. Select the class to generate in the browser Data package and click Generate in the class shortcut menu. For packages and components, you can also select **Generate**, but you will not be able to specify the generation options. All the options related to that task will be set according to the default values.

If you choose framework generation for a single class or for packages, the **Code Generation Options** dialog does not appear. The code is generated according to the default values.

If no errors occurred, you can view the results with your favorite file viewer or programming environment. Look for the files in the directory you specified as your **Working directory** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Code Engineering Sets' space='']**Round Trip Set** dialog or in the **Project Options** dialog. Additional sub-directories could be created.

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering' space='']

## PAGE 00874: (2026x Refresh1) Generating DARS report

- page_id: `304000214`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000214/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+DARS+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:54.391+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-av-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='']([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0' space='']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Summary and Overview' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3' space='']) DARS report, you must load the DARS template in these diagrams and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='load_dars_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the DARS report. [ATTACHMENT filename='Architectural_description_specification_window.png']
4. Click **Close**.

To generate DARS report use standard [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports' space=''] procedure.

## PAGE 00875: (2026x Refresh1) Generating DARS report in A1

- page_id: `303994126`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994126/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+DARS+report+in+A1
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) A1 Meta-Data Definitions
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-a1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in A1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='DARS_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To generate DARS report use standard [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports' space=''] procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000738 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00876: (2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports

- page_id: `304000738`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000738/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+document+based+reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:37.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Managing reports
- labels: ['create-report', 'quick-generate-report', 'report-wizard', 'generate-document-based-reports', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216474442

216474445

216474441

**On this page**

216474444

The modeling tool report handling functionality allows you to store UAF report data in a UAF project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. 
 UAFreports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. There is a report data element created by default in a project template for each UAF report. A report data element is filled in with predefined default data.

##### Creating a new report data element

You can create a new report data element from the shortcut menu of a viewpoint or view package. 
 
 To create a report data element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a package for which you want create a report data element .
2. Choose **Generate Report**, select a required report group and a report template. A new report data element that stores the default data for a particular report is created.

The newly created report data element is filled in with default data for successful generation of the report. To see variables and data of the report, expand the report element node in the Containment tree.

##### Changing the data stored within a variable

If you need to change data stored within a variable, open the variable's Specification window and then edit the **Value** property value. 
 
 You can store not only variables as data within a report data element, but the editable default report properties as well. The following table lists available editable default report properties and their descriptions.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | To specify the name of the report data element. |
| Generate Recursively | Set to true to generate the report, recursively including all inner packages within a defined report scope. |
| Data | To select one or more packages and / or elements to define a scope for generating the report. |
| Auto Image Size | To specify an image size in the report. |
| Empty Text | To specify a value to be inserted when data contains no value. |
| Image Format | To select an image format for all the images in the report. |
| Template | To select a report template.A report template is not a part of a project. |
| Variables | To add, remove, and order report input variables. |

To edit a report property, open the report data element's Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Generating a report from the report data

You can generate a report directly from the report data.

To generate a report from the report data element

1. Right-click a report data element and click **Quick** **Generate** **Report**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify the location to save the generated report.
3. Select whether or not to open the generated report directly after the generation.

You can create several reports with different data for each type of the report.

##### Generating a report using the Report Wizard

You can also use the **Report** **Wizard** for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.

To open the **Report** **Wizard**

- Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard** and select the report templates for the UAF views.

generated report directly after the generation. </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create several reports with different data for each rgb(51,51,51);">You can also use the <strong>Report</strong> <strong>Wizard</strong> for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.</span></p><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To open the <strong>Report</strong> <strong>Wizard</strong></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong> and select the report templates for the UAF views.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995651 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00878: (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard

- page_id: `303995651`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995651/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+Reports+from+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:44.885+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261051786

1261051787

1261051787

In addition to default document templates, Report Wizard allows you to create customized specification document templates. Templates may contain your own custom fields not related to model elements, as well as fields that correspond to model elements. Once customized, a Report Wizard template can be used with data from any project.

You can also format your template to create the style you want, including tables of contents, headers and footers, and page numbers. You can apply most character and paragraph formatting available from your rtf editor to the rtf template or specify with html tags in the HTML template, including keywords. Once the template has been completed, you can run the Report Wizard to create a report in a format that corresponds to the template.

Report Wizard templates can be in *txt, rtf, html, odt, odf, odp, docx, xlsx, pptx*, and *xml* for DocBook or FO files. If you prefer, you can work in the Report Wizard Template Editor until you have finished the template file and use the Report Wizard template windows to save it in the template folder. The default templates are located in the *<modeling tool home>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/data* folder.

All built-in rtf report templates are combined into the RTF (deprecated) report template category.

##### Synchronizing an attached template with a server project

You can [attach a local template](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Template+Management+Pane) to a server project. Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.

Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the locally attached template from a server project

1. Go to **Options > Environment** menu.

2. Select the **Report Wizard** pane.

3. In the Configure report properties table, set the **synchronizeAttachedTemplate** value to **true.**

This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.

To update the server project with a locally attached template

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='CM'].

2. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog.

3. Select the attached template and click **Open**.

4. Edit the attached template and click **Save**.

5. Click **Synchronize**.

6. Commit changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1261051785

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995619 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00880: (2026x Refresh1) Generating reports from the Containment tree

- page_id: `303995619`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995619/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+reports+from+the+Containment+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:53.945+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a Report Data in your model, you can generate a report directly from the Containment tree. You can right-click either Model or Report Data to generate it.

- .
- .

You must specify the template and data in the report data to generate a report from the **Report Data** in the Containment tree.

###### GenerateFromModel

To generate a report from Model in the Containment tree

1. Right-click a package in the Containment tree, select Generate Report , and then select a template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a report from Model in the Containment tree.

2. Select the save location and type the filename. A dialog will open asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer after the report generating process is complete.

3. Click either [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export' space=''] page to learn more.

###### GenerateFromReportData

To generate a report from Report Data in the Containment tree

1. Create a project.
2. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project . The Use Project dialog will open.
3. Select the predefined path: <install.root>\profiles** and the predefined profile: Report Profile
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Finish_button.png'] . The Report Profile will be added to your project in read-only mode.
5. Right-click Model and select Create Element .
6. Select **Report Data**. **Report Data** will be created under Model. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating report data in the Model in the Containment tree.
7. Right-click the Report Data in the Containment tree, and select Specification to open its Specification dialog.
8. Select a template file, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a template file for the Report Data using the Specification dialog.
9. Right-click the Class element in the Containment tree, and select **Quick Generate Report**. The **Select Location** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Generating a report from Report Data in the Containment tree.
10. Either create a new file, or select an existing one (the content of the generated report will override that of the selected file).

- To open the Report Wizard dialog and modify the data or output properties, click the Report Wizard on the shortcut menu.
- By default, the Quick Generate Report command will select the default settings from the last changes saved to the Report Data .

#### Glossary

- Model The package that is placed at the highest level of the Containment tree.

- Report data A collection of variables in a report in Report Wizard.

- Report template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about generating reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about generating reports using Report Wizard, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989732 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00881: (2026x Refresh1) Generating requirement reports

- page_id: `303989732`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989732/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+requirement+reports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:05.586+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can generate Requirement reports in the .docx and .html/.htm formats directly from your model.

##### Generating the Word document report

#### INFO: Report customization

Report customization

If you want to customize the report or create your own template for a requirement report, you may use the **[Report Wizard](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview)**.

****

To create a .docx format report by using the **Report Wizard**

1. In the Tools menu, click Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template area, select **Requirements**, and choose one of the following report type: 
-**Requirement Dependencies Report**. This report contains tables of requirement dependencies, that is separate tables for Copy, Derive, Refine, Satisfy, Trace, and Verify relations.-**Requirement Diagram**. This report lists all requirement diagrams of a selected scope. Diagram elements and dependencies are described in tables under each diagram.-**Requirement Report**. This is a standard detailed requirement report, containing all requirements attributes. This template may be used as a requirement specification report.-**Requirements Table Diagram Report**. This report lists all requirement tables of a selected scope.
3. Click**Next**.
4. In the Select Report Data area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click Next .
5. In the Select Element Scope area use:
  - The Add button to add an element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add All button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add Recursively button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove button in to remove the selected element from the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove All button in to remove all selected elements from the Selected objects area.
6. Click Next .
7. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
8. Click Generate . Your report is generated and automatically opens.

###### Generating requirement report from the Containment tree

To generate Requirement reports from the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the selected requirement specification (Package) or Requirement.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Generate Report > Requirements and select a desired requirement template.
3. In the Select Location dialog, specify a report location and specify a report file name.
4. Click Save . The report is generated.

##### Publishing projects to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloudallows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1.Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin' space='MCS'] in your modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.

You can republish a model each time you update it whilereacting to the reviewer's feedback. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.

To learn more, see the following topics:

#### NOTE: Publishing documents from synchronized projects

Publishing documents from synchronized projects

If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can generate Requirement reports in the .docx and .html/.htm formats directly from your model.</p><h3>Generating the Word document report</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91ef625e-776a-440b-91b3-83a82eaf0d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Report customization</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to customize the report or create your own template for a requirement report, you may use the <strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview">Report Wizard</a></strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To create a .docx format report by using the <strong>Report Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="966bebda-385e-4a0e-b813-718d6de9b27b">In the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="c54aa60e-7986-49e2-9315-06a53a9f55be"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Select Template area, select <strong>Requirements</strong>, and choose one of the following report type:<br /><span>- </span><strong>Requirement Dependencies Report</strong><span>. This report contains tables of requirement dependencies, that is separate tables for Copy, Derive, Refine, Satisfy, Trace, and Verify relations.<br /></span><span>- </span><strong>Requirement Diagram</strong><span>. This report lists all requirement diagrams of a selected scope. Diagram elements and dependencies are described in tables under each diagram. <br /></span><span>- </span><strong>Requirement Report</strong><span>. This is a standard detailed requirement report, containing all requirements attributes. This template may be used as a requirement specification report.<br /></span><span>- </span><strong>Requirements Table Diagram Report</strong><span>. This report lists all requirement tables of a selected scope.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="0fa760aa-97ee-4ccc-a03e-76a0f446f63e"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Click </span><strong>Next</strong><span>.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8e13d99f-709f-46b5-938f-cdb5d424c612">In the <strong>Select Report Data</strong> area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="9f2329f2-a9ce-4125-b2b7-f581e51c2925">In the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> area use:<br /><ul><li>The <strong>Add</strong> button to add an element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add All</strong> button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add Recursively</strong> button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove</strong> button in to remove the selected element from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove All</strong> button in to remove all selected elements from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d86a2d2a-a06f-41c8-a453-dfd7220319ee">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="df5d9150-5676-4ee8-aff7-5fb765fdbfbe">In the <strong>Output Options</strong> area, define the appropriate options.</li><li data-uuid="d7cbb87b-707f-47b8-837c-99dc9bbb083e">Click<strong> Generate</strong>.<br />Your report is generated and automatically opens.</li></ol><h4>Generating requirement report <span>from the Containment tree</span></h4><p><br /></p><p>To generate Requirement reports from the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="468243ce-73c5-43f9-bf68-2708d9e93aed">In the Containment tree, right-click the selected requirement specification (Package) or Requirement.</li><li data-uuid="3bbda056-3134-4901-896e-9094aa5e27df">From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Generate Report</strong> &gt; <strong>Requirements</strong> and select a desired requirement template.</li><li data-uuid="0d62e5fd-6db6-412d-bff8-6accf34e477b">In the <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog, specify a report location and specify a report file name.</li><li data-uuid="94406b3c-8384-40a6-a68c-56a27a459893">Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The report is generated.</li></ol><h3>Publishing projects to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">allows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1. </span>Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd518cb5-3be9-4ff7-9fc3-911d561a6cfb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="main_workflow_publishing.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;">Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can republish a model each time you update it while </span>reacting to the reviewer's feedback<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.<br /></span></p><p>To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f646a71d-6e9b-4f95-a76f-ab6b5b220655"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Publishing documents from synchronized projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross-cluster resource synchronization" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992404 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00882: (2026x Refresh1) Generating simulation files

- page_id: `303992404`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992404/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+simulation+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:55.841+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a simulation file, right-click the *Block* corresponding the system being simulated (see the image below). The shortcut menu displays the menu item **Tools******that contains**Export to Modelica** and **Export to Simulink**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To export to Modelica and Simulink

1. Depending on the selected option (either Export to Modelica or Export to Simulink ), do one of the following:
  - Specify options such as output directory, how to export unannotated SysML v1 properties, and whether diagram layout annotations should be generated on export. [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_export_options_.png']
  - Specify options such as format, output directory, S-Function or Simscape, Simscape port libraries, and whether to apply the <<SimulinkBlock>> stereotype. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_export_new_dialog (1).png']
2. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To generate a simulation file, right-click the <em>Block</em> corresponding the system being simulated (see the image below). <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2387eaaa-4a8e-4557-b468-104bdfdf01b6">The shortcut menu displays the menu item <strong>Tools</strong></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2387eaaa-4a8e-4557-b468-104bdfdf01b6"><strong> </strong>that contains<strong> Export to Modelica</strong> and <strong>Export to Simulink</strong>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_To_Simulink_And_Modelica.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To export to Modelica and Simulink</p><hr /><ol><li>Depending on the selected option (either <strong>Export to Modelica</strong> or <strong>Export to Simulink</strong>), do one of the following:<ul><li>Specify options such as output directory, how to export unannotated SysML v1 properties, and whether diagram layout annotations should be generated on export.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_export_options_.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify options such as format, output directory, S-Function or Simscape, Simscape port libraries, and whether to apply the &lt;&lt;SimulinkBlock&gt;&gt; stereotype. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_export_new_dialog (1).png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998417 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00883: (2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports

- page_id: `303998417`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998417/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+Use+Case+Description+Reports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:48.221+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Use Case Report Template

Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.

To generate a Use Case description document

1. Open the *Magic Library.mdzip* sample model from the directory. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.
2. Click Tools > Report Wizard to open the Report Wizard dialog.
3. Select a template, for example, **Default Template** > **Use Case (Simple),** and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. You can choose to: You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the **Report Variable** dialog.
  1. Select the built-in Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the Built-in Report Data in the Report Variable dialog by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='Variable_button.png'] or
  2. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create its report variables. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the Built-in report data. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a custom report data . [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Report Variable dialog.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The Select Element Scope option will open in the Report Wizard dialog.
6. Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip] model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.
7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The model package will be moved to the **Selected objects** tree on the right-hand side. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The output options pane will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.
9. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Browse_bigbutton.png'] to specify the location in which you want to save the report.
10. Select the file location, enter the filename, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is **.rtf*, the report file format will be **.rtf* as well.
11. Select the report image format, **.png*, **.jpg*, **.emf*, or**.wmf,*and select an image size option. TipIn some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select either the **Empty text** or **Custom text** option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.
13. Select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the report document with a default editor. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_button.png'] .

The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page [Default templates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Default+templates).

#### Glossary

- Use case A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.

- Model Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.

- Report data Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']

#### More Information on Generating Reports

You can generate different types of reports with the Report Wizard. To learn more, see [Web Publisher 2.0 Report](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Web+Publisher+2.0+Report).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use Case Report Template</span></h2><p>Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To generate a Use Case description document</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee750f38-d6c6-4241-9399-8afb53927165"><p>Open the <em>Magic Library.mdzip</em> sample model from the directory.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="magic-lib.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cafde055-4be2-48bd-9c97-db483f26c062"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="443bc5d5-b6c8-4c7a-a072-844b79dc9673">Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard </strong>to open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li data-uuid="79643997-d041-4951-8fdc-a2bad028dab7"><p>Select a template, for example, <strong>Default Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Case (Simple),</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseCase_template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li data-uuid="e75efa34-51d3-43ea-833a-09b4e7b3d358">You can choose to:<br /><ol><li>Select the <strong>built-in</strong> Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the <strong>Built-in</strong> Report Data in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />or</li><li><p>Click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create its report variables.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Built-in Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting the Built-in Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report-wizard-dialog-two.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the Built-in report data.</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting a Custom Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting a Custom Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rep-wiz-step-two.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting a custom report data .</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="The Report Variable Dialog" ac:alt="The Report Variable Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage-report-variables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Report Variable dialog.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3e62168-5bb4-403d-81cb-0df77361b8c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9fb4e513-bd26-4e88-99b4-2cc85e5af23d">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> option will open in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li data-uuid="3d106159-108c-449a-b187-640db8b4b747">Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the <em>MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip]</em> model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.</li><li data-uuid="20716567-14cd-42b6-8ce8-7d3bd199ef7b"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The model package will be moved to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> tree on the right-hand side.</p><h6><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Scope of the Report" ac:alt="Selecting the Scope of the Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-element-scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li data-uuid="ea66f5c3-77fb-46ef-abd9-9b233e1a7c7a"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The output options pane will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.<br /><br /></h6></li><li data-uuid="f5456283-b2d6-44b4-bfec-41e78cf40a71">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to specify the location in which you want to save the report.</li><li data-uuid="57781d70-1e5f-4549-b0ef-2d7c4a58d554"><p>Select the file location, enter the filename, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is <em>*.rtf</em>, the report file format will be <em>*.rtf</em> as well.</p></li><li data-uuid="d7f57bf1-f831-4000-a983-ffb4725bf5c7"><p>Select the report image format, <em>*.png</em>, <em>*.jpg</em>, <em>*.emf</em>, or<em> *.wmf, </em>and select an image size option.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78c70840-9f0c-4cda-8798-2fde5f502217"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The <strong>Display empty value as</strong> option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a7695818-3ef7-4b28-961f-f9a0980d2240"><p>Select either the <strong>Empty text</strong> or <strong>Custom text</strong> option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.</p></li><li data-uuid="5111774a-77ef-467a-a1eb-113c69538bd7"><p>Select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the report document with a default editor.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li data-uuid="61acf04c-7442-4889-b7fe-37b1bc374aa0">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Default+templates">Default templates</a>.</p><h2><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Glossary</span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="ebec7bd6-2ff7-45cf-b20b-d038b1eb42d6"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use case</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.<br /></span></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="7a9bfa41-a167-432e-b395-3423e009a28f"><strong>Model</strong><br />Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.</li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="9a230b0e-c270-440d-9361-95f06f647985"><strong>Report data</strong><br />Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.</li></ul><h2>Sample model</h2><p> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></p><hr /><h2>More Information on Generating Reports</h2><p>You can generate different types of reports with the Report Wizard. To learn more, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Web+Publisher+2.0+Report">Web Publisher 2.0 Report</a>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992389 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00884: (2026x Refresh1) Generating Word document report

- page_id: `303992389`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992389/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+Word+document+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:19.328+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Generating report documents
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a .docx format report

1. In the Tools menu, click Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template area, select SysML, and choose the report type. Click Next .
3. In the Select Report Data area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click Next .
4. In the Select Element Scope area use:
  - The Add button to add an element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add All button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add Recursively button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove button in to remove the selected element from the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove All button in to remove all selected elements from the Selected objects area.
5. Click Next .
6. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
7. Click Generate . Your report is generated and automatically opens. See the example: [ATTACHMENT filename='coverage_analysis.docx'] .

#### INFO: Report customization

Report customization

If you want to customize your report, use the **[Report Wizard](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Report Data</strong> area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> area use:<br /><ul><li>The <strong>Add</strong> button to add an element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add All</strong> button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add Recursively</strong> button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove</strong> button in to remove the selected element from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove All</strong> button in to remove all selected elements from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Output Options</strong> area, define the appropriate options.</li><li>Click<strong>Generate</strong>.<br />Your report is generated and automatically opens. See the example: <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="coverage_analysis.docx"><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined templates in Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91ef625e-776a-440b-91b3-83a82eaf0d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Report customization</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to customize your report, use the <strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview">Report Wizard</a></strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995256 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00885: (2026x Refresh1) Generic numbering mechanism

- page_id: `303995256`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995256/2026x+Refresh1+Generic+numbering+mechanism
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['numbering', 'numbering-scheme', 'numbering-customization', 'numbering-property', 'element-numbering', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show an element's place in a hierarchy.

With this feature you can easily:

- Customize currently applied numbering formats.
- Create and apply your own numbering formats.
- Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.

The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with generic numbering mechanism' space=''] describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog' space=''] explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.

Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Numbering customization | A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator . |
| Numbering property | Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering. |
| Numbering scheme | A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization. |
| Prefix | An affix placed before the element number. |
| Separator | A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme. |

**Related pages**

## PAGE 00886: (2026x Refresh1) Generic table

- page_id: `303994201`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1198506714

1198506717

1198506711

**On this page**

42

1198506716

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Generic Table gives you the ability to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place.

##### **Purpose**

A Generic Table displays a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place. Using Generic Tables enables you to:

- Edit property values of listed model elements.
- Create selected type elements.
- Create new derived properties or custom columns.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Generic Table project sample allows you to read about the Generic Table feature and analyze examples within that project sample. 
To open the sample, do either:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples > Diagrams > Generic table .
- Go to < the modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams and open the generic table.mdzip file.

All the examples given in this section are based on the data from this sample.

##### **Specific tasks in Generic Table**

All procedures dedicated only for Generic Table are as follows:

1199743192

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Table toolbars' space='']

1352031231

1352031232

1352031232

The Generic Table tool allows templates to:

- Access data of the Generic Table and other Table diagrams, e.g., Instance Table, Metric Table, and Glossary Table. See more samples in the tutorial.
- Use diagrams to get data from the Generic Table.
- Get the row elements.
- Get the column names.
- Get the cell values.
- Get column levels for the Generic Table tree view.
- Check for annotation values in table cells.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Dialog.

Like other Custom Tools, the Generic Table tool '*generictabletool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom Tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tool' space=''].*

To import the Generic Table tool to a template, for example, type the following code in the template.

```text

```

## PAGE 00888: (2026x Refresh1) Generic table tool API

- page_id: `303990196`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990196/2026x+Refresh1+Generic+table+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:16.886+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generic table tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### GenericTableTool diagram.

Class GenericTableTool:

Methods:

- +getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table
- +getTable( diagramName : String) : Table
- +closeTables() : Void

Class Table:

Methods:

- +getRows() : List<Element>

- +getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object

- +getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String

- +getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String
- +getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>
- +getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String
- +getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object
- +getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object
- +getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String
- +getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String

#### Getting generic table data

A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.

Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram elements

To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

**getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram element.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram names

To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.

**getTable( diagramName : String) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram name.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

#### Closing tables

Use the following method to close a table diagram.

##### Closing all diagram tables

**CloseTables() : Void**

```text

```

##### Closing a specific table diagram

**closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a table diagram you want to close.

##### Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name

**closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.

#### Getting row elements

The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.

##### Getting all row elements

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.

```text

```

##### Getting all row elements including collapsed

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.

```text

```

##### Getting all filtered row elements

**getFilteredRows() : List<Element>**

| $table.getFilteredRows() |
| --- |

The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.

| #foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows()) $row.name #end |
| --- |

The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Rows returned after filtering.

##### Getting row elements in specific row numbers

**getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.

The returned value is an Element.

#### Getting column names

Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.

The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.

##### Getting column names from column ID

**getColumn( columnId : String) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting column names from column numbers

**getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting all column names

**getColumnNames() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column names.

Example code:

Prints all column names.

```text

```

##### Getting all column IDs

**getColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column IDs.

Example code:

Print all columns IDs

```text

```

#### Getting cell values

A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.

##### Getting values from row element and column ID

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. 
Example code:

Prints the row, column, and value.

```text
$row.name 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]>
```

##### Getting values from row element and column name

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column name.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column number

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers

**getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column IDs as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column name as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element
- columnName – a column name

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column number as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.
- showFullType – the default value is true for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.

The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.

##### Getting cell value from returned object as string

**getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- object – an object returned from the getValue function.

The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.

For example:

```text

```

#### Getting visible column and cell values

A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.

##### Getting all visible column IDs

**getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.

##### Getting visible column names from column numbers

**getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a visible column.

##### Getting visible values from row elements and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row number and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row element and column number as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.
