<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995270 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00911: (2026x Refresh1) Hide Uncommon Elements filter

- page_id: `303995270`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995270/2026x+Refresh1+Hide+Uncommon+Elements+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.889+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1827084503

1827084505

1827084504

You can enable the *Hide Uncommon Elements* filter in the *Select Type* dialog to leave out uncommon element types and thus find the ones you need easier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Hide Uncommon Elements filter in the Select Type dialog

Once *Hide Uncommon Elements*is enabled, the**filter returns only those elements whose types are based on:

- Class (except Metaclass and Term)
- Component
- Signal
- Interface
- DataType
- Enumeration
- PrimitiveType
- AssociationClass

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specified property type

If the SysML profile is loaded, only elements of*Block*and*ValueType*types are listed in the *Select Type* dialog.

If the Stereotype tag types are being specified, metaclasses are not filtered out.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Metaclasses in the *Select Type* dialog

1827084502

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting port and part types' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993135 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00912: (2026x Refresh1) Hiding direction arrow on port shape

- page_id: `303993135`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993135/2026x+Refresh1+Hiding+direction+arrow+on+port+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:50.298+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x Refresh1) Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To hide the direction arrow on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'] shape

1. Right-click the Proxy Port or Flow Port shape and select the Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Port Direction property value to false.
3. Click Close . The direction arrow is hidden on the selected port shape.

The direction arrow of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'] shape is hidden by default. To display it, set the **Show Port Direction** property value to *true* in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To hide the direction arrow on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Port" /></ac:link> shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Proxy Port or Flow Port shape and select the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Port Direction</strong> property value to <em>false.</em></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The direction arrow is hidden on the selected port shape.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8be4db4-b70c-46f9-b22d-14251cd8a000"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The direction arrow of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Full Port" /></ac:link> shape is hidden by default. To display it, set the <strong>Show Port Direction</strong> property value to <em>true</em> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993029 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00913: (2026x Refresh1) Hiding the multiplicity

- page_id: `303993029`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993029/2026x+Refresh1+Hiding+the+multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:43.463+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x Refresh1) Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can hide the multiplicity next to the port andPart Property type.

To hide the multiplicity

1. Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape and select**Symbol Properties**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Multiplicity** property value to *false*.
3. Click **Close**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can hide the multiplicity next to the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">port and </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Part Property type</span>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To hide the multiplicity <br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Port" /></ac:link>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Full Port" /></ac:link></span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /></ac:link></span>, or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link></span> shape and select<strong> Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, set the <strong>Show Multiplicity</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997857 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00914: (2026x Refresh1) Hierarchy Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `303997857`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997857/2026x+Refresh1+Hierarchy+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:39.437+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Model Visualizer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard** collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.

To create a diagram using the **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Hierarchy Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33df9e1d-7457-4043-ac81-6afd9c3c69da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a diagram using the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995949 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00916: (2026x Refresh1) How aspects match with parts and ports

- page_id: `303995949`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995949/2026x+Refresh1+How+aspects+match+with+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:59.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is *Electrical,*which matches a port stereotype name W*Electrical.* When these conditions are met, you can display only the *Electrical* part of your model in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space='']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects' space='']>]]>

If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] with the same name as the aspect's and [apply it for parts and ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype). Or you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom aspects' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Matching the diagram aspect *Electrical* with the port stereotype name *Electrical*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is <em>Electrical, </em>which matches a port stereotype name W<em>Electrical.</em> When these conditions are met, you can display only the <em>Electrical</em> part of your model in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display parts and port according to aspect >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704f5b7-46ac-40ca-a191-e433fd556328"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the same name as the aspect's and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype">apply it for parts and ports</a>. Or you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom diagram aspect]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspects_and_stereotype_match.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How aspects match with parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Matching the diagram aspect <em>Electrical</em> with the port stereotype name <em>Electrical</em>.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998361 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00917: (2026x Refresh1) How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context

- page_id: `303998361`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998361/2026x+Refresh1+How+modeling+tool+adds+its+elements+to+velocity+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:33.772+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358598954

1358598955

1358598955

In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.

All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as "Class" during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to **$Class** (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the **#foreach**, **#forrow**, **#forcol**, or **#forpage** directive, followed by the **#if** directive to search for the name of the class.

This section will use part of the "*Inventory Control System.mdzip*" file in *<install.root>/samples/casestudies* as an example to show you how to reference elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case View.

You need to know the elements inside the "Use Case View" package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template' space='']*.

The "Use Case View" package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing **"$Actor**," **"$UseCase**," and **"$Diagram**" respectively.

In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.

1358598953

**Related pages**

In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.</p><p>All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as &quot;Class&quot; during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to <strong>$Class</strong> (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the <strong>#foreach</strong>, <strong>#forrow</strong>, <strong>#forcol</strong>, or <strong>#forpage</strong> directive, followed by the <strong>#if</strong> directive to search for the name of the class.</p><p>This section will use part of the &quot;<em>Inventory Control System.mdzip</em>&quot; file in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/samples/casestudies</em> as an example to show you how to reference elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Use Case and Actor" ac:alt="Use Case and Actor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavb053991ffb02adecec8c94bef76403bd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Use Case View.</h6><p>You need to know the elements inside the &quot;Use Case View&quot; package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating your first template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>The &quot;Use Case View&quot; package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing <strong>&quot;$Actor</strong>,&quot; <strong>&quot;$UseCase</strong>,&quot; and <strong>&quot;$Diagram</strong>&quot; respectively.</p><p>In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5863349b-5f1c-4533-8b27-111ca2bfbf72"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598953</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8804a6f4-8982-4de8-b9e7-353a297adadb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997035 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00918: (2026x Refresh1) How to avoid triggering element recovery

- page_id: `303997035`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997035/2026x+Refresh1+How+to+avoid+triggering+element+recovery
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:45.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning > (2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity > (2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:

1. Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.
2. Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).
3. Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.
4. Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions

To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , set the Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects property value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inspection

To select the relevant level of project composition inspection

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , specify the Composition inspection option value:
  - Select Standard if you need the active validation to search for issues only in the project, but not in the projects it uses.
  - Select Advanced if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:</p><ol><li>Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.</li><li>Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).</li><li>Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.</li><li>Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="avoid_recovery.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions</h6><p>To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong> Composition Inspection</strong>, set the <strong>Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="detect_illegal_model_references_in_shared_projects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb30e2f-6a0b-40da-97d1-2126e8b29b05"><ac:parameter ac:name="">inspection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To select the relevant level of project composition inspection</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong>Composition Inspection</strong>, specify the <strong>Composition inspection</strong> option value:<ul><li>Select <strong>Standard</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues <em>only</em> in the project, but not in the projects it uses.</li><li>Select <strong>Advanced</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000369 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00919: (2026x Refresh1) How to migrate

- page_id: `304000369`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000369/2026x+Refresh1+How+to+migrate
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:25.313+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='CM'].

.

If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:

- Cyclic usage
- Indirect usage

If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receivea warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.

For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying suspicious usages' space='MCS']. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.

If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one **in the same order they are listed in the message**. Repeat the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015ab40b-16ae-4330-a8ed-746e3ac71725"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div>You are suggested to migrate each time you open a project created with the older version of UPDM / UAF with the modeling tool with the latest UAF version<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></div><div><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_question_new.png.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></div><div><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eeac4a9-7b3f-4916-b856-df9bd9a159ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Cyclic Usages"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Identifying suspicious usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cyclic Usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning cycle.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.</p><p>If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. Repeat the migration.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="question2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="263" ac:width="807"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>  </p><div><p><br /></p></div><div><p class="auto-cursor-target">More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:</p></div><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

## PAGE 00920: (2026x Refresh1) HTML editor

- page_id: `303995328`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995328/2026x+Refresh1+HTML+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:27.508+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

630043998

630044000

630043999

You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.

To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML

Do one of the following:

- Select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Text Box' space=''] shape and click the HTML button when Switch To HTML Text appears on the lower-left corner of the shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_text_to_html.png']

- Draw the note/text box, using the Note(HTML text) or Text Box(HTML text) buttons on the diagram palette.

Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar

You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.

To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog

- Simply click to select the HTML check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box:
  - Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] :
    1. Open an element's specification window.
    2. Click the Documentation/Comments tab.
    3. Check HTML. Click Close . See the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window

- In the Model Browser:
  1. Click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'] .
  2. Click the **Documentation** tab.
  3. Click **HTML**, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser
- Element Specification window:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. Click the Tags property group, when editing a tagged value.
  3. Click HTML . Then click Close . See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='customer_user_is_added.png'] HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value
- In the **To Do** field:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. In the dialog, click the**To Do** field to edit it.
  3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to open the **To Do** text dialog.
  4. In the **To Do** text dialog, click **HTML**. Edit the text as you wish, then click **OK** and click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog

You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.

To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value

1. Click the HTML button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.
2. On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

630043997

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993205 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00921: (2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model

- page_id: `303993205`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993205/2026x+Refresh1+Humidifier+example+to+Simulink+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:14.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Humidifier*example model in Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier system sample' space='']Learn more about *Humidifier*sample model >>

You can find the *Humidifier*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Humidifier.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Humidifier**.*

To simulate the *Humidifier System* model

1. Export the HumidifierSystem Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: S-Function version 2
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported Humidifier Simulink files. They are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files.png']
6. Double-click the file HumidifierSystem.slx . The model open in the Simulink window. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_window.png']
7. On the SIMULATION tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='library_browser_button.png'] .
8. In the Simulink Library Browser dialog, select expand the Simulink group and select Sinks . See the figure below.
9. Drag and drop the Scope block onto the HumidifierSystem Simulink model. See the figure below.
10. Select signal line that connects the humidity-out port of the room block to the humidity-in port of the humidifier block and click on the Scope block. See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_scope_block.png']
11. On the SIMULATION tab, click the arrow on the PREPARE area and select the Model Settings . [ATTACHMENT filename='model_settings.png']
12. In the Configuration Parameters dialog, on the left side of the dialog select a Solver and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the the Simulation time group change Start time to 0.0 , Stop time to 3600 . - In the Solver selection group select Type Variable-step or any other desirable solver that is suitable. - Click the arrow of the Solver details group and change the Max step size to 0.1 . [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog.png']
13. Press Apply > OK .
14. Double-click the Scope block in the model. An empty black plot opens.
15. Click button. In the Configuration Properties: Scope dialog, select the Logging tab and clear the Limit data points to last box. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_properties.png']
16. Press Apply > OK .
17. In the Scope plot toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and wait for the simulation results in the plot. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The model open in the <strong>Simulink</strong> window.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="71"><ri:attachment ri:filename="library_browser_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li><li>In the <strong>Simulink Library Browser</strong> dialog, select expand the <strong>Simulink</strong> group and select <strong>Sinks</strong>. See the figure below.</li><li>Drag and drop the Scope block onto the <strong>HumidifierSystem </strong>Simulink model. See the figure below.</li><li>Select signal line that connects the<em> humidity-out</em> port of the room block to the<em> humidity-in</em> port of the<strong> </strong>humidifier block and click on the Scope block. See the figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_scope_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click the arrow on the <strong>PREPARE</strong> area and select the <strong>Model Settings</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Configuration Parameters</strong> dialog, on the left side of the dialog select a  <strong>Solver </strong>and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: <br /><strong>  </strong>-<strong> </strong>In the the <strong>Simulation time</strong> group change <strong>Start time </strong>to <strong>0.0</strong>, <strong>Stop time </strong>to <strong>3600</strong>.<br />  - In the <strong>Solver selection</strong> group select<strong> Type </strong><strong>Variable-step</strong> or any other desirable solver that is suitable.<br />  - Click the arrow of the <strong>Solver details</strong> group and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.1</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="configuration_parameters_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Press<strong> Apply</strong> &gt;<strong> OK</strong>.</li><li>Double-click the <strong>Scope</strong> block in the model. An empty black plot opens. </li><li>Click button. In the <strong>Configuration Properties: Scope </strong>dialog, select the <strong>Logging </strong>tab and clear the <strong>Limit data points to last </strong>box.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="configuration_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Press <strong>Apply </strong>&gt; <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Scope plot toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Run_button_matlab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and wait for the simulation results in the plot.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulation_results_plot.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996873 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00922: (2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model R2016a

- page_id: `303996873`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996873/2026x+Refresh1+Humidifier+example+to+Simulink+model+R2016a
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:31.810+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models > (2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the Block TestCaseHumidifier::HumidifierSystem and select Tools > Export to Simulink.
2. Set the options listed below and click OK :
  - **Format**: XML (.sdl)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: S-Function version 2
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display two generated Simulink “.slx” files. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on the file named Environment.slx . The model will open up in a Simulink window. Rearrange the blocks to reflect the signal flow arrowhead directions. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Open the Library Browser either by using the 4-block icon or by going to Tools > Library Browser . Find and click on Sinks among the list in the Libraries panel, which will be categorized under Simulink . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Pull-and-drag the Scope block from the Sinks library list into the Environment Simulink model. Attach a signal line segment from the Scope port onto the signal line that connects the humidity-out port of the room block to the humidity-in port of the humidifiersystem block. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Go to Simulation > Model Configuration Settings . This allows selecting the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a Start Time of 0 , and a Stop Time of 3600 . Under Solver Options , select the solver Type to Variable-step (or any other desirable solver that is suitable) and change the Max step size to 0.01 . Press Apply , then press OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Double-click the Scope block in the model. An empty (black) plot will show up. Press the ‘Scope’ parameters button (the gear icon above the empty plot). A ‘Scope’ parameters dialogue box will pop up. Go to the History tab and uncheck the option box Limit data points to last . Press Apply , and then OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Go to Simulation > Run . Double-click on the Scope block to see the simulation results. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Go to the <strong>History</strong> tab and uncheck the option box <strong>Limit data points to last</strong>. Press <strong>Apply</strong>, and then <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configuring_the_Scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>. Double-click on the <strong>Scope</strong> block to see the simulation results.<br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulation_Results_for_Environment_Model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier example to Simulink model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992467 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00923: (2026x Refresh1) Humidifier System example

- page_id: `303992467`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992467/2026x+Refresh1+Humidifier+System+example
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:58.526+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Humidifier*example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier system sample' space='']Learn more about *Humidifier*sample model >>

You can find the *Humidifier*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Humidifier.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\Humidifier.

To simulate the *Humidifier System* model

1. Export the HumidifierSystem Block to the Modelica file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modelica export' space=''] page.
2. Download and install Dymole in your system. To learn more, refer to the Dymola page.
3. Launch the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select the saved *HumidifierSystem**.mo* file from your directory. The Humidifier System model is displayed in the **Package Browser**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the Graphics tab toolbar, click Check to check for syntax and semantic errors in the model. The Logs panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [ATTACHMENT filename='Check Button.png']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: 
 a. Select the******Simulation**tab and in its toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
b. In the **Simulation Setup** dialog, specify the **Start time******value to **0** and the **Stop time******value to**3600**seconds. 
c. Click **OK**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click **Simulate**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *HumidifierSystem*variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see the input and output of the EnvironmentModel , variables from the Variable Browser must be chosen. Selecting EnvironmentModel > _Environment > room > humidity_out will display the humidity level of the room overtime on the plotting window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Select the<strong> </strong><strong>Simulation </strong>tab and in its toolbar click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="setup_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier System example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    b. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulatio Setup.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier System example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the<strong> Simulation</strong> tab toolbar, click <strong>Simulate</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulate Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Humidifier System example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0ad6689-6aa0-43a3-b678-89d3d32e2be3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><em>HumidifierSystem </em>variables are displayed in the <strong>Variable Browser</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To see the input and output of the</span><strong> EnvironmentModel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, variables from the </span><strong>Variable Browser</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> must be chosen. Selecting </span><strong>EnvironmentModel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>_Environment</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>room</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>humidity_out</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> will display the humidity level of the room overtime on the plotting window.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993276 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00925: (2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example

- page_id: `303993276`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993276/2026x+Refresh1+Hydraulics+example
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:18.466+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Hydraulics* example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics sample' space='']Learn more about *Hydraulics*sample model >>

You can find the *Hydraulics*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Hydraulics .
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Hydraulics**.*

To simulate the *ConnectedTanks* model

1. Export the ConnectedTanks Block to Modelica file. How to >>
2. Make sure the Dymola tool is installed. How to >>
3. Start the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select saved ConnectedTanks .mo file from your file directory. A ConnectedTanks model is displayed in the Package Browser . [ATTACHMENT filename='connectedtanks_model_in_package_browser.png']
5. In the Graphics tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='check_syntax_button.png'] to check model for syntax and semantic errors. The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_syntax_connectedTanks.png']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: a. Select the Simulation tab and in its toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='setup_button.png'] . b. In the Simulation Setup dialog, specify the Start time value to 0 and the Stop time value to 120 seconds (or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 120 seconds should be enough) . c. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='simulation_setup_connectedTanks.png']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *ConnectedTanks***variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see the input and output of the ConntectedTanks model , select variables in the**Variable Browser**as follows: - ConnectedTanks > _ConnectedTanks > fluidReservoir1 > fluidLevel . The changing fluid level of the first tank is displayed on the **Plot** window. See the following figure. - ConnectedTanks > _ConnectedTanks > fluidReservoir2 > fluidLevel . Thechanging fluid level of the second tank is displayed on the **Plot**window. See the following figure. [ATTACHMENT filename='plot_diagram_fluid_level.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="check_syntax_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Setup the simulation run for the model:<br />    a. Select the<strong> </strong><strong>Simulation </strong>tab and in its toolbar click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="setup_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons_dymola" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />    b. In the <strong>Simulation Setup</strong> dialog, specify the <strong>Start time</strong><strong> </strong>value to <strong>0</strong> and the <strong>Stop time</strong><strong> </strong>value to<strong> 120 </strong>seconds <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 120 seconds should be enough)</span> .<br />    c. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulation_setup_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the<strong> Simulation</strong> tab toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons_dymola" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fd7759b-b761-4020-9824-4cc9a459d300"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the <em>ConnectedTanks</em><em> </em>variables are displayed in the <strong>Variable Browser</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To see the input and output of the</span><strong> </strong><em>ConntectedTanks </em>model<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">select variables in the </span><strong>Variable Browser </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">as follows:</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span><strong> </strong>-<strong> ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>_ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>fluidReservoir1</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>fluidLevel</strong>. The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> changing fluid level of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">first tank is displayed</span> on the <strong>Plot</strong> window. See the following figure.<br /></span><strong> </strong>-<strong> ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>_ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>fluidReservoir2</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt;</span><strong> fluidLevel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> changing fluid level of the second tank is displayed on the <strong>Plot </strong>window. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">See the following figure.</span></span></span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="plot_diagram_fluid_level.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993176 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00926: (2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model

- page_id: `303993176`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993176/2026x+Refresh1+Hydraulics+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:14.964+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Hydraulics* example modelin Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics sample' space='']Learn more about *Hydraulics*sample model >>

You can find the *Hydraulics*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Hydraulics .
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Hydraulics**.*

To simulate the *Hydraulics* model

1. Export the ConnectedTanks Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported *Hydraulics* model Simulink files and choose the +ConnectedTanksLibrary folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files_connectedTanks.png'] ssc_build
6. In the Command Window type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) and press Enter . This generates a folder sscprj in the +**ConnectedTanksLibrary** directory as well as a **ConnectedTanksLibrary**_lib.slx file in the directory with the original ConnectedTanks .slx file. [ATTACHMENT filename='command_window_connectedTanks.png']
7. Go back to the file directory, select the Hydraulics package with exported Simulink files and in the Current Folder select the ConnectedTanks.slx . [ATTACHMENT filename='current_folder_connectedTanks.png']
8. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on ConnectedTanks .slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='hydraulics_model_simscape.png']
9. On the **SIMULATION** tab, click the arrow on the **PREPARE** area and select the **Model Settings**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. In the **Configuration Parameters** dialog, on the left side of the dialog: 
a. Select a **Solver**property group****and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: 
****-****In the the **Simulation time** group, change **Start time**to **0.0**, **Stop time**to **200**(or any other reasonable number of seconds). 
 - In the **Solver selection** group, select**Type****Variable-step** or any other desirable solver that is suitable. 
 - Click the arrow of the **Solver details** group and change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. b. Select Simscape property group and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the Data Logging group, change Log simulation data to All . - Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog_connectedTanks.png']
11. Press Apply > OK .
12. In the ConnectedTanks Simulink window, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
13. On the main toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open a Simscape Results Explorer : ConnectedTanks window where you can expand the ConnectedTanks blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. For the tanks fluidReservoir1 and fluidReservoir2 , the properties fluidLevel can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation. To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot. [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_explorer_connectedTanks.png']
14. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the ConnectedTanks Simulink model. A Block Parameters dialog opens where you can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters. [ATTACHMENT filename='fluiReservoir2_block_prameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div><p>This page describes how to simulate the <em>Hydraulics</em> example model<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> in Simulink</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics sample" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <em>Hydraulics </em>sample model &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4365f6e2-9efb-4b06-8ce8-7209854932d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can f<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ind the <em>Hydraulics </em>sample model in:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the modeling tool: Welcome window &gt; Samples &gt; Simulink and Modelica Transformation &gt; Hydraulics .</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Installation directory: </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\<em>Hydraulics</em><em>.</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To simulate the <em>Hydraulics</em> model</p><hr /><ol><li>Export the <em>ConnectedTanks </em>Block to Simulink file. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Simulink+export#Simulinkexport-ExportingmodeltotheSimulinkfile" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>In the <strong>Simulink Export Options</strong> dialog select the following options:<br /><ul class="diff-block-target"><li><p><strong>Format</strong>: XML (.slx)</p></li><li><p><strong>S-Function or Simscape</strong>: Simscape</p></li><li><strong>Simscape port libraries</strong>: Create new port types</li><li><strong>Composite Signals</strong>: Bus Creators/Selectors</li></ul></li><li>Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.</li><li>Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.</li><li>In the file directory select the location of your exported<em> <em>Hydraulics </em></em>model Simulink files and choose the <em>+ConnectedTanksLibrary</em> folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="file_directory_simulink_files_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>ssc_build</li><li>In the <strong>Command Window</strong> type <strong>ssc_build</strong> (or<strong> sscbuild</strong> depending on the Matlab version) and press <strong>Enter</strong>. This generates a folder <strong>sscprj</strong> in the <strong>+<strong>ConnectedTanksLibrary </strong></strong>directory as well as a<strong> <strong>ConnectedTanksLibrary</strong>_lib.slx</strong> file in the directory with the original <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong><strong>.slx</strong> file.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="command_window_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go back to the file directory, </span>select the <em>Hydraulics </em>package with exported Simulink files and in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> select the <em>ConnectedTanks.slx</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current_folder_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong><strong>.slx</strong> to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hydraulics_model_simscape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click the arrow on the <strong>PREPARE</strong> area and select the <strong>Model Settings</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Configuration Parameters</strong> dialog, on the left side of the dialog:<br />a. Select a  <strong>Solver </strong>property group<strong> </strong>and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /><strong>  </strong>-<strong> </strong>In the the <strong>Simulation time</strong> group, change <strong>Start time </strong>to <strong>0.0</strong>, <strong>Stop time </strong>to <strong>200 </strong>(or any other reasonable number of seconds).<br />  - In the <strong>Solver selection</strong> group, select<strong> Type </strong><strong>Variable-step</strong> or any other desirable solver that is suitable.<br />  - Click the arrow of the <strong>Solver details</strong> group and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>.<br /></span>b. Select <strong>Simscape</strong> property group <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /></span>  - In the <strong>Data Logging</strong> group, change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>.<br />  - Make sure <strong>Limit data points</strong> is unchecked.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Press<strong> Apply</strong> &gt;<strong> OK</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> Simulink window, click<strong> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Run_button_matlab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</strong></li><li>On the main toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="64"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot.</span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_explorer_connectedTanks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the<strong> ConnectedTanks Simulink</strong> model. A <strong>Block Parameters</strong> dialog opens where you can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fluiReservoir2_block_prameters.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></div><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00927: (2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a

- page_id: `303996817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996817/2026x+Refresh1+Hydraulics+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model+R2016a
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:25.823+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models > (2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the *Block* **Data:ConnectedTanks** and select **Tools**> **Export to Simulink.**
2. Set the options listed below and click **OK**:
  - **Format**: XML (.sdl)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - **Simscape port libraries**: Create new port types
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display the generated files.
5. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. In the **Current Folder** panel, double-click on the folder named**+ConnectedTanksLibrary_create** (this sets the current directory to this folder). Type **ssc_build** in the**Command Window** and press Enter. This will generate a folder **sscprj** in the **+ConnectedTanksLibrary_create** directory as well as a **ConnectedTanksLibrary_create_lib.slx** file in the directory with the original **ConnectedTanks.slx** file. Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the **Current Folder** panel, double-click on **ConnectedTanks.slx** to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Go to **Simulation** > **Model Configuration Settings**. This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a **Start Time** of **0**, and a **Stop Time** of **200** (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. Under **Solver Options**, keep the solver **Type** to **Variable-step** (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press **Apply**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. In Model Configuration Settings, select **Simscape** in the**Commonly Used Parameters** panel. Change **Log simulation data** to **All**. Make sure**Limit data points**is unchecked. Press **Apply**. Press **OK**. [ATTACHMENT filename='Model_Configuration_Settings.png']
10. In the ConnectedTanks Simulink window, go to Simulation > Run .
11. Once the model has compiled, go to the Matlab’s command window and type/enter ssc_explore(simlog) (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function ssc_explore , or a file ssc_explore.m is in the same directory as ConnectedTanks.slx in the Current Folder panel). A Simscape data logging explorer: Connected Tanks window will open up. [ATTACHMENT filename='Ssc_explore(simlog)_command_and_the_Simscape_data_logging_explorer.png']
12. In the Simscape data logging explorer , the ConnectedTanks blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the tanks fluidReservoir1 and fluidReservoir2 , the properties fluidLevel can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the Data cursor icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen (by clicking it). [ATTACHMENT filename='Using_the_Simscape_data_loggng_explorer.png']
13. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the ConnectedTanks Simulink model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 7 through 10 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing_block_parameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ConnectedTanks_Simulink_Simscape_Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Model Configuration Settings</strong>. This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a <strong>Start Time</strong> of <strong>0</strong>, and a <strong>Stop Time</strong> of <strong>200</strong> (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>. Press <strong>Apply</strong>.<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Simscape_Model_Configuration_Settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In Model Configuration Settings, select <strong>Simscape</strong> in the<strong> Commonly Used Parameters</strong> panel. Change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>. Make sure<strong> Limit data points </strong>is unchecked. Press <strong>Apply</strong>. Press <strong>OK</strong>.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model_Configuration_Settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> Simulink window, go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>.</li><li>Once the model has compiled, go to the Matlab’s command window and type/enter<strong> ssc_explore(simlog)</strong> (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function <strong>ssc_explore</strong>, or a file <strong>ssc_explore.m</strong> is in the same directory as <strong>ConnectedTanks.slx</strong> in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel). A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed.
