<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996715 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 00929: (2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks in texts

- page_id: `303996715`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996715/2026x+Refresh1+Hyperlinks+in+texts
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:18.540+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can link text directly to internal or external resources by adding hyperlinks. This enables you to open a document, wizard, diagram, or other [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) by clicking the hyperlink.

##### Places to add hyperlink in text

You can add a hyperlink in the following places:

- In all elements of the text format (e.g. Note, Anchor, Comment).
- In all element property values of the text format (e.g. **To Do**, **Documentation**, **Body**, **Body** **and Language**, **Text**,**Min**,**Max**). You can insert a hyperlink only for LiteralString and OpaqueExpression [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED'] (e.g **Default Value** property, **Value** property, and **Argument** property values).
- In all element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property groups.

##### Adding hyperlinks in text

Before adding a hyperlink, select the text format, either HTML or Plain text. If you select:

- HTML text - you can add any type of hyperlink . >]]>
- Plain text - you can add a hyperlink only to an element/symbol. >]]>

###### all typesAdding a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

To add a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

1. Select a place to add a hyperlink. [All places to add hyperlinks in text >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks+in+texts#Hyperlinksintexts-Placestoinserthyperlinkintext)
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_hyperlink.png'] or press Ctrl+K.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] , select the tab according to hyperlink type.
4. Specify the boxes and options in the selected tab. Descriptions of boxes and tabsPlease see the following for more in-depth information about the boxes and options on each tab: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Address tab' space='']**Address** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) File tab' space='']**File** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Symbol tab' space='']**Element/Symbol** tab. 
 - [**OSLC Link** tab.](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&version=2026x Refresh1)
5. Click **OK**.The hyperlink added in text becomes underlined and blue. For example, the figure below shows where the hyperlink on the text "Car" will open the Specification window of the *Car* Class. The hyperlinked text will be represented with icon and will be automatically updated after changes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

###### elementAdding hyperlink in text to element/symbol

Two additional ways to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:

- 
- 

****

###### **drag**Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.

To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] , select one or more elements.
2. Drag the selected elements onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED'] or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Text Box' space=''] shapes on the diagram pane. 
The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue. The **Update Mode** and **Text to display** values are selected according default settings. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >>

###### **guards**Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:

- A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.
- A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.
- A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MED']

To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event

1. Select one of the following:
  - guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.
  - guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.
  - State Invariant in Sequence diagram.
  - Change Event expression in State Machine diagram.
2. Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+****Backspace. If you press Ctrl+K, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Makesure the selected property is correct.
3. Select the property from the list. The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior. The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the **Update Mode** and **Text to display** list boxes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >> For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the *currentSpeed* Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the *currentSpeed* Property to *speed*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) HTML editor' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can link text directly to internal or external resources by adding hyperlinks. This enables you to open a document, wizard, diagram, or other <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes">hyperlink type</a> by clicking the hyperlink.</p><h3>Places to add hyperlink in text</h3><p>You can add a hyperlink in the following places:</p><ul><li data-uuid="af5f912d-1e7b-4978-b085-e8fda91efbb2">In all elements of the text format (e.g. Note, Anchor, Comment).</li><li data-uuid="ae4bdac7-b209-489a-ae23-c7eb2b36cd65"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In all element property values of the text format (e.g. <strong>To Do</strong>, <strong>Documentation</strong>, <strong>Body</strong>, <strong>Body</strong> <strong>and Language</strong>, <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Text</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">,<strong> Min</strong>,<strong> Max</strong></span>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07175de9-8db7-4ca7-87ff-972ae977e385"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can insert a hyperlink only for LiteralString and OpaqueExpression <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Value Specifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g <strong>Default Value</strong> property, <strong>Value</strong> property, and <strong>Argument</strong> property values).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="63cf119d-55e3-460f-b74c-52a4b31d9890">In all element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> property groups.<br /><br /></li></ul><h3>Adding hyperlinks in text</h3><p>Before adding a hyperlink, select the text format, either HTML or Plain text. If you select:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6cd71d3a-447e-4784-8de0-3f59d038972b">HTML text - you can add any <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes">type of hyperlink</a>. <ac:link ac:anchor="all types"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to add hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="127a6187-1b5e-4672-8327-86104624df73">Plain text - you can add a hyperlink only to an element/symbol. <ac:link ac:anchor="element"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to add hyperlink in text to element/symbol >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da78d0a3-43fd-41ca-89b3-a00b8e60d5d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">all types</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Adding a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type</h4><p>To add a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7d5ebd6e-a366-4174-93bc-37cd2de6e727">Select a place to add a hyperlink. <span class="confluence-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks+in+texts#Hyperlinksintexts-Placestoinserthyperlinkintext">All places to add hyperlinks in text &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="d7363de5-6de6-4156-96a2-edbd60051b34">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="insert_hyperlink.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks in texts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or press Ctrl+K.</li><li data-uuid="2593c0eb-f372-4d1e-8470-767d92500367">In the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink data-uuid="58225830-004a-4e95-a5f1-cb88d909f6d6"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the boxes and options in the selected tab.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dde09e0-007e-4786-b3bf-e59617ec3572"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Descriptions of boxes and tabs</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please see the following for more in-depth <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996656 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 00930: (2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks on elements

- page_id: `303996656`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996656/2026x+Refresh1+Hyperlinks+on+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:17.608+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hyperlinked elements or diagrams allow you to quickly navigate through the model, to other resources, or between them. If an element or diagram refers to the , you can access that content by double clicking an element or an entire diagram. You can add all [types of hyperlinks](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) on elements and diagrams.

##### Adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

To add a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

1. Select the element or diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator [IMAGE alt='' src='']**Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
  - From the shortcut menu, choose **Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
3. The **Specification** window opens. Choose the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, and click the **Add** button.
4. In the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog, select a tab according to the [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) you want to insert.
5. Specify the boxes and options according to the selected tab. The meaning of boxes and options of each tab is described in the following pages: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Address tab' space='']**Address** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) File tab' space='']**File** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Symbol tab' space='']**Element/Symbol** tab 
 - [**OSLC Link** tab](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&version=2026x Refresh1)The **Text to display** box does not exist in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
6. Select the **Active** check box to activate the hyperlink. Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. You can also:
  - Click the **Open** button, to open the content of the hyperlink, e.g., a webpage or another external resource, a file on your filesystem, or an element/symbol.
  - Click the **Edit** button, to edit an existing hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Add** button, to add a new hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Remove** button, to delete the hyperlink. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **OK**. 
The hyperlink is added on the selected element or diagram. In the Model Browser, you will see the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hyperlinks/Go To icon on the element. On the diagram pane, on the symbol, you will see the icon representing the subject the hyperlink is added to. You can instantly open a hyperlink by double clicking an element or diagram.

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

In the Model Browser, you will see the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlinkElementAdornment.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks on elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Hyperlinks/Go To icon on the element. On the diagram pane, on the symbol, you will see the icon representing the subject the hyperlink is added to. You can instantly open a hyperlink by double clicking an element or diagram.</p></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="db317641-094c-4871-a9bc-9b2c57eb49db"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li data-uuid="cd33e016-4e7e-47a9-a337-89dc3c335978"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li><li data-uuid="9d40a51e-c0bf-4a86-8c5f-a1ecfec0bf86"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DEVG/?contextKey=managing-hyperlinks&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Managing hyperlinks</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00931: (2026x Refresh1) Identifying Package Dependencies

- page_id: `303995567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995567/2026x+Refresh1+Identifying+Package+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:46.914+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In a model, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED'] can be associated with other packages or model elements in a project scope or outside the project scope. In other words, packages can have relations with external elements in used projects, shared libraries, profiles, etc. An element depends on the used project when at least one of its metaproperties reference elements from at least one share of that used project. In this case, the element depends on that used project.

To avoid any violations on editing a package, especially on removing or exporting a package to another project, it is always good to know all of the package dependencies. Dependency analysis allows users to detect cyclic dependencies that impede reusing the package in the current or other projects because it can cause an infinite recursion. This analysis manages the impact of changes to other related elements.

In the modeling tool, the Dependency Checker allows you to analyze package dependencies in the whole project scope or analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages. The dependency severity level status shows how a package depends on a particular element. There are three severity levels:

- Info . The lowest severity.
- Warning . Conflicts that require your attention are displayed.
- Error . The highest severity.

#### NOTE: Severity levels

Severity levels

While checking dependencies, conflicts of the selectedseverity (and higher) are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependencies** panel.

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog, you can define the default values for checking dependencies. You can specify the following:

- The Check for Cyclic Dependencies among Used Projects option. Click to select true if you want the cyclic dependencies to be checked while exporting or sharing the packages.
- The Dependency Checker Severity Level option. Specify the severity level. The dependencies of the specified severity (and higher) will be displayed.
- The Ignore Standard/System Profiles option. Click to select true if you want to exclude the standard/system profiles from checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.

Each time you start the dependency checking, you can change the default settings.

In the following procedures, learn how to work with the Dependency Checker:

- 
- 
- 
- 

setDefaultTo set default values for the Dependency Checker

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General option in the option group list.
3. Click the **Dependency Checker** option. The list of options opens.
4. In the Dependency Checker options list, set the desired values and click **OK** when you are done.

#### NOTE: Description of an option

Description of an option

Each option has a short description. Click the option to see the description in the description area of the dialog.

analyzeWholeTo analyze package dependencies of the whole project or between the selected package/model and shared packages

1. Choose one of the following:
  - To analyze package dependencies of the whole project, on the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Dependency Checker**.
  - To analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages, right-click the package or model in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, point to **Tools**and then click**Dependency Checker**.
2. The Dependency Checker dialog opens. Select the Minimal Severity from the list, check ignore or not standard and system profiles while checking and click OK to execute the dependency checker. If some dependencies are found, they are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependencies** panel .

analyzeExportTo identify package dependencies while exporting packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Export Packages to New Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to export. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exporting packages to new projects' space=''] .
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have **Error** and **Warning** severity levels to be displayed.

analyzeSharingTo identify package dependencies while sharing packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Share Packages . The Shared Packages dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to share. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sharing project data' space=''].
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have**Error**and**Warning**severity levels to be displayed.

#### INFO: Cyclic dependency

Cyclic dependency

If there is a chain of dependencies such that A → M(1), M(1) → M(2), M(2) → M(3), ..., M(X) → M(A), where:

- A is an element from the used project M(A)
- M(1..X) are other used projects
- A → M(x) is element A dependency on used project M(x)
- M(y) → M(x) is a dependency of at least one element in used project M(y) on used project M(x),

then this chain is called a cyclic dependency and every atomic dependency in this chain is considered as part of cyclic dependency.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999438 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00932: (2026x Refresh1) Identifying relationships

- page_id: `303999438`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999438/2026x+Refresh1+Identifying+relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:56.456+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Transformations > (2026x Refresh1) ER to SQL (generic / Oracle) transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Identifying relationships are transformed in the same way as in the UML to SQL transformation, that is, the foreign key of the transformation gets to be included into the primary key of the dependent entity (the one at the multiple end of the relationship). The difference in ER to SQL transformation case is that the ER model eliminates guessing, which relationships are identifying and which ones are not. UML to SQL transformation guesses, which UML associations should be identifying, by using a heuristic method - composition associations are treated as identifying (this heuristic is controlled by the **Treat compositions as identifying**transformation property). In ER models, identifying relationships are explicitly marked as such, hence there is no need to guess (See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Identifying relationships and dependent entities' space=''] that specifies how identifying relationships is modeled).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Identifying relationships are transformed in the same way as in the UML to SQL transformation, that is, the foreign key of the transformation gets to be included into the primary key of the dependent entity (the one at the multiple end of the relationship). The difference in ER to SQL transformation case is that the ER model eliminates guessing, which relationships are identifying and which ones are not. UML to SQL transformation guesses, which UML associations should be identifying, by using a heuristic method - composition associations are treated as identifying (this heuristic is controlled by the <strong>Treat compositions as identifying </strong>transformation property). In ER models, identifying relationships are explicitly marked as such, hence there is no need to guess (See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Identifying relationships and dependent entities" /></ac:link> that specifies how identifying relationships is modeled).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998591 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00933: (2026x Refresh1) Identifying relationships and dependent entities

- page_id: `303998591`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998591/2026x+Refresh1+Identifying+relationships+and+dependent+entities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:46.497+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

One-to-many and (very rarely) one-to-one relationships can be declared as “identifying” relationships. An identifying relationship is a “stronger” version of the relationship, indicating that the entity at the multiple end of the relationship cannot exist without the entity on the other end.

You can create these relationships using the buttons on the diagram pallet, as well as turn an existing relationship into an identifying one and back again. To do this, either change the **Is Identifying**property value in the relationship Specification window or select the appropriate check box on its shortcut menu.

An identifying relationship is drawn as a solid line. Non-identifying relationships use heavy dashes.

Dependent / independent entities are a closely related concept. Dependent entities occur at the multiple end of the identifying relationship. They cannot exist without the independent entity at the other end. In addition, every inherited entity (if you are doing ER modeling) is considered to be dependent. A dependent entity's primary key includes the other entity's key as part. This is implied, not shown in the model. Dependent entities are automatically recognized and drawn with rounded corners.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of identifying relationship and dependent entity in the ER diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>One-to-many and (very rarely) one-to-one relationships can be declared as “identifying” relationships. An identifying relationship is a “stronger” version of the relationship, indicating that the entity at the multiple end of the relationship cannot exist without the entity on the other end.</p><p>You can create these relationships using the buttons on the diagram pallet, as well as turn an existing relationship into an identifying one and back again. To do this, either change the <strong>Is Identifying </strong>property value in the relationship Specification window or select the appropriate check box on its shortcut menu.</p><p>An identifying relationship is drawn as a solid line. Non-identifying relationships use heavy dashes.</p><p>Dependent / independent entities are a closely related concept. Dependent entities occur at the multiple end of the identifying relationship. They cannot exist without the independent entity at the other end. In addition, every inherited entity (if you are doing ER modeling) is considered to be dependent. A dependent entity's primary key includes the other entity's key as part. This is implied, not shown in the model. Dependent entities are automatically recognized and drawn with rounded corners.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2017-9-8_16-26-22.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Identifying relationships and dependent entities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of identifying relationship and dependent entity in the ER diagram.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998286 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00934: (2026x Refresh1) Image Library tab

- page_id: `303998286`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998286/2026x+Refresh1+Image+Library+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:20.410+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Image formats

INLINE

The**Image Library** tab displays images grouped in logical sets. You can use a bundled image collection or add your own set of images.

To open the **Image Library** tab

- From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.

To add your own set of images into **Image Library**, do either

- Compress a folder (.zip) with a set of desired images and place it in the modeling tool's installationdirectory: *\data\imagelibraries\commonsymbols*. Image formatsYou can add images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
- Create an Image Library plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Writing plugins' space='DEVG'] page.

Using the **Image Library** tab, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying image' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Image Library** tab consist of three components:

##### Quick filter

You can search for images in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- Pattern Search: type * to find any image. For example, type "*Bank" if you want to find "Bank" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Image Library tree

You can drag an image from the **Image Library** tree directly:

- On the symbol (image is applied for symbol).
- To the diagram pane (image shape is created).

##### Image Library Preview area

You can preview the image before applying it on the symbol in the **Image Library** Preview area.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e37a7ae8-dbfd-4227-b41c-98152bd73815"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<strong> Image Library</strong> tab displays images grouped in logical sets. You can use a bundled image collection or add your own set of images.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Image Library</strong> tab</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Window</strong> menu, select <strong>Image Library.<br /></strong>The<strong> Image Library</strong> tab opens on the right side of the window.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add your own set of images into <strong>Image Library</strong>, do either</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Compress a folder (.zip) with a set of desired images and place it in the modeling tool's <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">installation </span>directory: <em>\data\imagelibraries\commonsymbols</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7133a2c-8cd5-4931-bfe9-bef0c9f1c5b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Image formats</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Create an <strong>Image Library</strong> plugin. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Writing plugins" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Using the <strong>Image Library</strong> tab, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying image" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[apply an image on symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Image Library tab" ac:alt="Image Library tab"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library_tab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Image Library tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Image Library</strong> tab consist of three components:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="222645bb-3d77-41fc-9a8e-18e3559b8e96" /></p><p><br /></p><h3>Quick filter</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can search for images in the <strong>Whole hierarchy</strong> or <strong>Recently used</strong> tabs using one of the following way:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Pattern Search: type * to find any image.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a760ea5-36bd-430a-95a4-e3c65e3ea8f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;*Bank&quot; if you want to find &quot;Bank&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h3>Image Library tree</h3><p>You can drag an image from the <strong>Image Library</strong> tree directly:</p><ul><li>On the symbol (image is applied for symbol).</li><li>To the diagram pane (image shape is created).</li></ul><h3>Image Library Preview area</h3><p>You can preview the image before applying it on the symbol in the <strong>Image Library</strong> Preview area.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996393 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00935: (2026x Refresh1) Image Shape

- page_id: `303996393`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996393/2026x+Refresh1+Image+Shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:46.180+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

490233696

490233698

490233697

The image shape provides a simple and quick way to insert a image into a diagram. Images may include logos, graphs, tables, or others. The default shape size after the insertion is the actual image size.

You can format image shape representation properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols' space='']**Symbol Properties**dialog.

To insert an image on the diagram pane

1. On the diagram palette, in the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 12.58.27 PM.png'] Image Shape button or press the I key.
2. Click on the diagram pane. The Open dialog opens.
3. Browse for an image in the *.gif, *.jpg, *.jpeg, *.svg, or *.png format and click Open .

490233695

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996049 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00936: (2026x Refresh1) Image tool

- page_id: `303996049`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996049/2026x+Refresh1+Image+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:21.177+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267909359

1267909360

1267909360

$image provides a rich set of image manipulation methods that enable you to transform images during report generation. With **$image**, you can scale, rotate, split, and resize images, include external images, and export them for TXT and HTML templates.

**Syntax**

These manipulation methods can be broadly divided into seven categories:

1. Scaling images
2. Rotating images
3. Splitting images
4. Resizing fixed-pixel images
5. Resizing fixed-measurement images
6. Including external images
7. Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates
8. Limiting image size

Multiple image transformations will have cumulative effects. Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. While the parameters for resizing an image with a specific measurement (in, cm, mm, pt, or px) must be specified in a pair of quote marks, all other size parameters do not need to be specified in a pair of quote marks.

The **keepRatio** parameter takes either a true or false value; a true value will resize the image and keep the original image height/width ratio; a false value will only resize one axis, as set by the method name, resulting in a stretched image.

1267909358

**Related pages**

Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. While the parameters for resizing an image with a specific measurement (in, cm, mm, pt, or px) must be specified in a pair of quote marks, all other size parameters do not need to be specified in a pair of quote marks.</p><p>The <strong>keepRatio</strong> parameter takes either a true or false value; a true value will resize the image and keep the original image height/width ratio; a false value will only resize one axis, as set by the method name, resulting in a stretched image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0e833e7-926f-4a24-bee8-36421e59fb28"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267909358</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b0bb516e-26b0-4363-a404-fddac23bdb9f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['impact-analysis-relation-maps', 'node-role-impact-analysis-map', 'node-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-role-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-impact-analysis-map', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216907718

216907721

216907719

Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.

The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:

- Operational Role Impact Analysis Map
- Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Role Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Impact Analysis Map

Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.

The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

216907717

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating predefined relation map' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying criteria through the legend' space='']

1335007736

1335007764

1335007753

###### Description

The Impact Matrix describes the mapping between the Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements and Strategic Elements using the Impacted By relationship directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

###### Implementation

The rows of the matrix are:

- Operational Elements (Operational Activity and Operational Architecture)
- Resources Elements (Resource Architecture)
- Services Elements (Service Architecture)
- Strategic Elements (Actual Outcome, Capability, and Actual Strategic Phase)

The columns of the matrix are:

- Strategic Elements (Challenge, Opportunity, and Enterprise Goal)

###### Sample

1335007735

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000185 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00939: (2026x Refresh1) Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `304000185`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000185/2026x+Refresh1+Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:53.434+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['implementation-matrix', 'mapping-between-systems-operational-and-service-elements', 'nodes/performers-implementation-matrix', 'operational-activities-implementation-matrix', 'operational-exchanges-implementation-matrix', 'ov-sv-gap-analysis', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1370353206

1370353209

1370353207

**Description**

The **Implementation Matrix** describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elementsdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].The implementation in UAF is defined by the Implements relationship. To specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps, the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UAF.

**Implementation**

An Implementation Matrix is represented by a Dependency Matrix-based diagram.

The Rows of the matrix are:

- Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.
- Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.
- Service Elements: Service Interface.
- Summary & Overview Elements: Architecture.

The Columns of this matrix are:

- Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.
- Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.
- Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.
- Resource Elements: Resource Connector.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**Predefined configurations**

The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.

Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking **Analyze** > **OV-SV Gap analysis**.

There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:

- Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.
- Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.
- Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.

These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.

1370353205

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System in UAF' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04acb29b-665f-465b-a68a-486e7603c579"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353206</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f540b0-f4c2-4e30-b269-446935501054"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353209</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a39ff5af-3b72-45d6-898c-663fe78d3675"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353207</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Implementation Matrix</strong> <span>describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary &amp; Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elements </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>The implementation in UAF is defined by the Implements relationship. To specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps, the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UAF.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Implementation Matrix is represented by a Dependency Matrix-based diagram.</p><p>The Rows of the matrix are:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service Elements: Service Interface.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Summary &amp; Overview Elements: Architecture.</span></span></li></ul><p>The Columns of this matrix are:</p><ul><li>Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.</li><li>Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.</li><li>Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.</li><li>Resource Elements: Resource Connector.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Implementation Matrix" ac:alt="Implementation Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Implementation Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Predefined configurations</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45c0cb87-2f2d-4406-96ab-d5a253e8e5e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>OV-SV Gap analysis</strong>.</p><p>There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:</p><ul><li>Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.</li><li>Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.</li><li>Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.</li></ul><p>These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32508abf-c16f-449c-a106-1b7e23db9cd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353205</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="System in UAF" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="100aa383-ba7c-4685-b1d3-2b06ffa80227" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995445 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00940: (2026x Refresh1) Implementing or Overriding Operations

- page_id: `303995445`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995445/2026x+Refresh1+Implementing+or+Overriding+Operations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:31.627+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

When you inherit Classes from the base Class that has abstract functions, you have to redefine them in the inherited Classes. The implement/override operations tool will help you generate operations that are defined as abstract in the base Class.

The**Implement/Override Operations**command can be invoked in two cases:

- When one classifier inherits operations from the base classifier ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case study #1: Adding a rule to create a Generalization relationship on drag-and-drop' space='UPDG'] ).
- When some classifiers implement an Interface ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Forward traceability - realization' space=''] ).

To start the Implement/Override Operations****tool

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH'] of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering .
2. Select Implement/Override Operations.
3. The Select Operations to Implement/Override dialog box opens.

| Box | Function |
| --- | --- |
| All | Contains names of all operations defined within the selected class. |
| Selected | Contains the selected operations. |
| > | Moves the selected operation from the All list to the Selected list. |
| < | Moves selected operation from the Selected list to the All list. |
| >> | Moves all operations from the All list to the Selected list. |
| << | Moves all operations from the Selected to the All list. |
| OK | Generates operations that are in the Selected list. |
| Cancel | Exits the dialog box without any changes. |
| Help | Displays help. |

#### TIP: Shortcut Key

Shortcut Key

Double-click the item name and it will be moved to the opposite list.

**Related Pages**

- Tools
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']
- Class diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case study #1: Adding a rule to create a Generalization relationship on drag-and-drop' space='UPDG']
- Realization relationship

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991360 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00941: (2026x Refresh1) Implied Connectors

- page_id: `303991360`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991360/2026x+Refresh1+Implied+Connectors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:40.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The implied relation functionality allows you to analyze existing relations among parts in the Internal Block Diagrams. It let you know that parts are somehow related with each other without giving the detailed view of actual Connectors and ports. You can display implied relations between parts which are connected through ports/interfaces. By default, the implied relation is represented as a dashed line with a short summary in label of how many actual connectors are underneath. You can specify a different style of the implied relation by using its the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

The implied relation functionality allows you to:

- Display implied relations between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] through ports/interfaces.
- Display actual [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] and ports from the implied relations level by level or all at once.
- Find actual Connectors in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] directly from the implied relation.
- Specify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space=''] of the implied relation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On the left side: the implied relation shows that 18 connectors exist between TV and TV Tuner parts. On the right side: all 18 actual connectors and ports are shown by using the Semantic Zoom In, or Display Underlying Connectors command.

How to work with implied Connectors read in the following pages:

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Property path notation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990936 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00942: (2026x Refresh1) Import

- page_id: `303990936`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990936/2026x+Refresh1+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:13.213+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Permission with the stereotype XSDimport. The permission client must be the schema class stereotypes*«XSDschema*» Component, supplier namespace Package *XSDnamespace*.

- namespace maps to the supplier name.
- annotation maps to the UML Attribute documentation.
- schemaLocation maps to the TaggedValue.

#### PANEL: import XML representation summary

import XML representation summary

```text
<import
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
namespace = anyURI
```

```text
schemaLocation = anyURI
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>	Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</import>
```

In the following example, the same namespace may be used both for real work, and in the course of defining schema components in terms of foreign components.

#### PANEL: The treatment of references as internal references to the names being defined in a schema document

The treatment of references as internal references to the names being defined in a schema document

```text
<schema xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" targetNamespace="uri:mywork" xmlns:my="uri:mywork">
```

```text
<import namespace="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"/>
```

```text
<annotation>
```

```text
<documentation>
```

```text
<html:p>[Some documentation for my schema]</html:p>		</documentation>
```

```text
</annotation>
```

```text
…
```

```text
<complexType name="myType">
```

```text
<sequence>
```

```text
<element ref="html:p" minOccurs="0"/>		</sequence>
```

```text
…
```

```text
</complexType>
```

```text
<element name="myElt" type="my:myType"/>
```

```text
</schema>
```

The treatment of references as ·**QNames**· implies that since the target namespace and the XML Schema namespace differ (with the exception of the schema for schemas), then without massive redeclaration of the default namespace either the internal references to the names being defined in a schema document or the schema declaration and definition elements themselves must be explicitly qualified. This example shows the first option, while most other examples in this specification showed the second.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']import UML model example.

#### PANEL: import XML code sample

import XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com"›
```

```text
<xs:import namespace = "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" schemaLocation = "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" />
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Permission with the stereotype XSDimport. The permission client must be the schema class stereotypes<em> «XSDschema</em>» Component, supplier namespace Package <em>XSDnamespace</em>.</p><ul><li>namespace maps to the supplier name.</li><li>annotation maps to the UML Attribute documentation.</li><li>schemaLocation maps to the TaggedValue.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb3f5623-ac5b-4d0c-a277-0009751a54b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">import XML representation summary</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;import</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	namespace = anyURI</pre><pre>	schemaLocation = anyURI</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;<br />	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/import&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the following example, the same namespace may be used both for real work, and in the course of defining schema components in terms of foreign components.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa4f9a1c-bdf8-47e7-99fd-e0638c333905"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">The treatment of references as internal references to the names being defined in a schema document</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;schema xmlns=&quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; xmlns:html=&quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml</a>&quot; targetNamespace=&quot;uri:mywork&quot; <br />xmlns:my=&quot;uri:mywork&quot;&gt;</pre><pre><br />	&lt;import namespace=&quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml</a>&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre><br />	&lt;annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;html:p&gt;[Some documentation for my schema]&lt;/html:p&gt;<br />		&lt;/documentation&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/annotation&gt;</pre><pre>	…</pre><pre>	&lt;complexType name=&quot;myType&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;sequence&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;element ref=&quot;html:p&quot; minOccurs=&quot;0&quot;/&gt;<br />		&lt;/sequence&gt;</pre><pre>		…</pre><pre>	&lt;/complexType&gt;</pre><pre><br />	&lt;element name=&quot;myElt&quot; type=&quot;my:myType&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The treatment of references as ·<strong>QNames</strong>· implies that since the target namespace and the XML Schema namespace differ (with the exception of the schema for schemas), then without massive redeclaration of the default namespace either the internal references to the names being defined in a schema document or the schema declaration and definition elements themselves must be explicitly qualified. This example shows the first option, while most other examples in this specification showed the second.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 139 import UML Model Example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>import UML model example.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e860fb9d-f856-475e-bc7f-9088444b01eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">import XML code sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:schema xmlns:nm = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot; xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot;›</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:import namespace = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtmln">http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml&quot;</a> schemaLocation = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml</a>&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999587 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00944: (2026x Refresh1) Import Maps migration from CSV Import Plugin

- page_id: `303999587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999587/2026x+Refresh1+Import+Maps+migration+from+CSV+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:30.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['import-map-migration', 'show-excel-csv-import-migration-message', 'csv-import-map-migration', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [Excel/CSV Sync](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to table) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space=''] (import to model) features.

All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:

To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin

1. Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.
2. In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map**** or Import Using Existing Map Group .
3. In the **CSV Import Map Migration** message, select **Yes**. To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the **Do not ask again** check box is clear.
4. In the **Select Import Map** or **Select Map Group**dialog, select Import Map or Map Group.
5. Click OK . The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. Select the file, check mapping options. How to >>
7. Click the **Import** button. 
The data is imported. A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.

If the **CSV Import Map Migration** message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the **Project Options** dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.

To enable the **CSV Import Map Migration** message

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the search box of the Project Options dialog, type " mig ".
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message option value to true to enable the functionality.
4. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files">Excel/CSV Sync</a> (import to table) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import to model) features.</p><p>All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:</p><p><br /></p><p>To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.</li><li>In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map<strong> </strong></strong>or <strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message, select <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d34b7df2-dfb8-43dd-aa7f-895a7782a5b7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the <strong>Do not ask again</strong> check box is clear.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> or <strong>Select Map Group </strong>dialog, select Import Map or Map Group. </p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> opens. </li><li>Select the file, check mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data is imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="903c2d06-1978-4902-a2a1-54dc3404e22c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>If the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the search box of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, type &quot;<em>mig</em>&quot;.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999264 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00945: (2026x Refresh1) Import syntax

- page_id: `303999264`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999264/2026x+Refresh1+Import+syntax
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:50.321+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Import tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

715763235

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

715763237

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

715763236

To use the Import tool syntax

1. To declare Import tool in the template, type #import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool') in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The #import directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.
  - The first parameter 'import' is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, 'import' , but it can be named anything).
  - The second parameter 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool' has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.
2. To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:

- $import.include('child.docx') to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or
- $import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A') to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.

****

**$import**

This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.

****

**$import.include(FileName)**

**FileName** in [$import.include(FileName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1) specifies the location of a file in the file system. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The **FileName** parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

****

**$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**

**FileName** in [$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2) functions the same as in the sub-section above. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. **SectionName** denotes a paragraph in the document named **FileName**, which is delimited by the **#sectionBegin(SectionName)** and **#sectionEnd** identifiers. The **SectionName** variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

#### INFO: Information

Information

You can also use **$attachedFile**in the parameter of **$import.include(FileName)** and **$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>#import</strong> directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.<br /><ul><li>The first parameter<strong> 'import'</strong> is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, <strong>'import'</strong>, but it can be named anything).</li><li>The second parameter <strong>'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool'</strong> has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.</li></ul></li><li>To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:    </li></ol><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A')</strong> to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import</strong></p><p>This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1">$import.include(FileName)</a> specifies the location of a file in the file system. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The <strong>FileName</strong> parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2">$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</a> functions the same as in the sub-section above. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. <strong>SectionName</strong> denotes a paragraph in the document named <strong>FileName</strong>, which is delimited by the <strong>#sectionBegin(SectionName)</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong> identifiers. The <strong>SectionName</strong> variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd51469a-e548-4f4f-83ef-ac7e3af3ab2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use <strong>$attachedFile </strong>in the parameter of <strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong> and <strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998629 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00946: (2026x Refresh1) Import tool

- page_id: `303998629`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998629/2026x+Refresh1+Import+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:49.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1175109811

1175109813

1175109812

Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.

The Import tool allows you to:

- Parse and import RTF , DOCX, HTML , and text templates from any location in the file system
- Reuse the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes #sectionBegin and #sectionEnd , introduced with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] .

1175109810

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43a306aa-5cf3-487d-b5ab-7dc55be2e4b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109811</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5906bd29-43fe-4769-ba0e-a0555c875317"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109813</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9338f24e-8903-4191-82d2-3e615044c314"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109812</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.</p><p>The Import tool allows you to:</p><ul><li>Parse and import <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX, HTML</em>, and text templates from any location in the file system</li><li>Reuse the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong>, introduced with the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#includeSection Directive]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c24317f-6306-485b-83a6-79ca6155bab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="02c30e2f-93e2-4da8-9cd7-9b065513f43f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
