<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993989 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00961: (2026x Refresh1) Importing projects from Ecore files

- page_id: `303993989`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993989/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+projects+from+Ecore+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:03.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. 
 
To import an Ecore file

1. In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.
2. Use the Importing projects from MOF files procedure.

This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To import an Ecore file</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.</li><li>Use the <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Importing+projects+from+MOF+files">Importing projects from MOF files</a> procedure.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32276341-4f32-43be-be16-e7105068a796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992148 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00962: (2026x Refresh1) Importing projects from MOF files

- page_id: `303992148`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992148/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+projects+from+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:37.412+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) MOF support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import a project from a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Import From > MOF XMI File .
2. In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click Open . The file is imported as a separate project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To import a project from a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong>.</li><li>In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click <strong>Open</strong>. The file is imported as a separate project.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998633 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00963: (2026x Refresh1) Importing relationships

- page_id: `303998633`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998633/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:23.212+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files > (2026x Refresh1) Import examples
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Main conditions

You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For thisthe following conditions must be met:

- 
  - The source/client and target/supplierelements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.
  - The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space=''].
  - The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.

##### Preparing the Excel file to import relationships

When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The target column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship. In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>

If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:

- Select to search elements and references in the Target Scope Only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog when importing data.
- Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.

##### Relationships import procedure

To import relationships

1. Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. >]]>
2. Open the project in the modeling tool. Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] functionality.
4. In the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog , select the Excel file. How to >>
5. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the relationship type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
6. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.[Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
8. Click the Import button. The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']>]]>

The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:

- The relationship end is empty in the data file.
- The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.

In the following example, you can see specified import options in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The import of Satisfy relationship.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- Saving an Import Map
- Managing Map Groups
- Excel and CSV Import dialog
- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea"> Learn more about mapping area &gt;&gt;</a></p></li><li data-uuid="c5eb9fbe-6204-48ff-a06f-5e1796c54566">(Optional) Click the<strong> Save Map</strong> button to save your map options as the Import Map. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Saving+an+Import+Map">Learn more how to save Import Map &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="a8ed08f1-e400-4986-ae34-ab8fcf8ca10d">Click the <strong>Import</strong><span> button.<br /></span>The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify <strong>Identification Property</strong> value</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> or <strong>I</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>dentify Relationship (End) Source by</strong> and <strong>Identify Relationship (End) Target by</strong> values. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about mapping options in the Excel/CSV Import dialog>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b36b6e-52a8-475f-92cc-71a4f243e323"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="6010d621-7113-41f8-908b-bf33cceb6169"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The relationship end is empty in the data file.</span></li><li data-uuid="5c61116c-9957-4e70-8a90-6342160076b6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the following example, you can see specified import options in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="result_of_relationships_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The import of Satisfy relationship.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="f11b0090-f4eb-4ef5-a455-f9c5f314c926"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing data from Excel or CSV files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="5e0575a7-8ec4-4394-8266-e065ee41472c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Saving+an+Import+Map" class="current">Saving an Import Map</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="1caecd53-5725-4691-bcb5-d963df23ba93"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Managing+Map+Groups" class="current">Managing Map Groups</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="76b2d164-7b9c-4cdd-a8d9-6871a1773bbb"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog" class="current">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="72458b2c-df78-4cb9-ae30-4925b52e3bf6"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files" class="current">Sync with Excel or CSV files</a></p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992295 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00964: (2026x Refresh1) Importing ReqIF files

- page_id: `303992295`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992295/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+ReqIF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:50.496+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Importing requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

818054109

INLINE

818054112

INLINE

**On this page**

43

818054111

INLINE

##### Importing ReqIF files

You can import ReqIF files from:

- IBM DOORS 9.4, 9.5, and 9.6
- IBM DOORS Next Generation
- PTC Integrity
- Polarion REQUIREMENTS
- Siemens Teamcenter
- Dassault Systemes Reqtify

You can import any ReqIF file – with or without specifications.

To import a ReqIF requirement file

1. Open a project wherein you want to import requirements.
2. From the File menu, select Import From > Requirement Interchange Format (ReqIF) File .
3. In the opened **Load** dialog, browse for your ReqIF file and click **Open**.
4. The ReqIF file is imported.

If Requirements in the ReqIF file have ID prefixes, they are set automatically after import only for imported Requirements. Manage ID prefixes when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Requirements numbering' space=''].

800700

When the ReqIF file is imported, a notification message appears, stating the quantity of imported or updated elements. If you import a ReqIF file without specification, the message doesn't contain the line that indicates new or updated specifications. You also see no option **Show imported specification table(s)**. 
 
If you imported a ReqIF file with the specification, you can view the list of imported elements. To do that, click the **Show imported specification table(s)** link in the opened notification message. The list is displayed in a generic table format.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change import options, click the **You can change import and mapping options here** link in the opened notification message.

The notification message is displayed for a fixed duration, but you can find all project notification messages in the Notification Window. To open the Notification Window, click **Window** > **Notification Window**, or press Ctrl + M.

Imported requirements are presented in the **Containment** tree. The requirement specification is represented as a model package and requirements are represented as model elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there are images or files attached to the ReqIF file, they are marked with the «AttachedFile» stereotype and placed in a separate package as model elements after the import.

All images in the *BMP* file format are automatically converted to the *PNG*format.

If there are any deleted elements in the source file, they are automatically marked with the «deprecated» stereotype once imported onto the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing requirements importing options

Changing requirements importing options is beneficial if you have a custom requirement type defined, or you are importing another type of data instead of requirements, for example, test cases.

A requirement type is limited to class and class extended types.

You can change the following import options, among others:

- Specify an element type to create for the objects you are importing;
- Specify a relationship type to create for the relationships you are importing;
- Specify a path to the resource location;
- Choose whether to import all properties into the model or not and form a list of ignored and not imported properties;
- Choose which properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated';
- For a full list, see the 'To change ReqIF file importing options' procedure or the ReqIF section in the Project Options table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space=''] table.

To change ReqIF file importing options

We recommend changing importing options before requirements are imported and no changes are made in the project, because after you have changed importing options, you need to remove the requirements you have imported and re-import it.

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Click a desired option specification cell to change it's value.
4. Click OK after you are finished.

##### Updating requirements

It is common to modify requirements after importing them. Importing requirements in a ReqIF format allows updating the requirements present in the model without any data loss – all changes are identified. 
 
To update ReqIF file by importing a new file

1. Open a project wherein you want to update requirements.
2. Import a ReqIF file with updated requirements.

To update requirements from the project

1. Open the project containing imported requirements.
2. In the Containment tree, select a requirement specification package and open it's shortcut menu.
3. Click Tools > ReqIF > Update From Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File .
4. In the opened Load dialog, browse for your ReqIF file and click Open . The ReqIF file is updated.

After requirements have been updated, you get a notification message informing about an update status. If you update a ReqIF file without specification, the message doesn't contain the line that indicates new or updated specifications. You also see no option **Show imported specification table(s)**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
In the change log table, you can see the detailed list of updated elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
During update, no elements are removed, only new elements are added and statuses of all elements are updated. Elements not present in an updated ReqIF file are marked as obsolete.

##### Managing obsolete requirement elements

In the model, obsolete elements are marked with an exclamation mark in a yellow triangle. A «deprecated» stereotype is applied to such requirement elements automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
**Removing Obsolete Elements** 
In the project, you can choose to remove only the selected obsolete element or all obsolete elements at a time. To remove one selected obsolete element, use the that is described in the following section. 
 
To remove all obsolete elements

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the requirements specification package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > ReqIF > Remove Obsolete Elements .

****

**Searching for obsolete elements**

#validationTo search for obsolete elements

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the requirements specification package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Validation > Obsolete Elements and one of the following:

- 
  - Remove Obsolete Elements – removes the selected obsolete elements from a project
  - Ignore – ignores obsolescence from the element and leaves it in the model
  - Select in Validation Results – opens the Active Validation Results panel wherein you can perform appropriate validation actions.

To remove one selected obsolete element, use the <ac:link ac:anchor="validation"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validating procedure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that is described in the following section. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To remove all obsolete elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Containment</strong> tree, right-click the requirements specification package.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>ReqIF</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove Obsolete Elements</strong>. </li></ol><p><strong> </strong></p><p><strong>Searching for obsolete elements</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54bb4677-4d0f-43f2-aab5-3db2a64661ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#validation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To search for obsolete elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Containment</strong> tree, right-click the requirements specification package.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Obsolete Elements</strong> and one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Remove Obsolete Elements</strong> – removes the selected obsolete elements from a project<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li><strong>Ignore</strong> – ignores obsolescence from the element and leaves it in the model<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /></span></li><li><strong>Select </strong><strong>in</strong><strong> Validation </strong><strong>Results</strong> – opens the <strong>Active</strong><strong> Validation</strong><strong> Results</strong> panel wherein you can perform appropriate validation actions.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1978d6d7-7e29-40a0-aa0b-071b8dc33c03"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">818054108</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cc68c6e1-2b04-4b80-bf7d-409c8f5de624" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992319 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00965: (2026x Refresh1) Importing ReqIF files from the command-line interface

- page_id: `303992319`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992319/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+ReqIF+files+from+the+command-line+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:54.142+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Importing requirements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing ReqIF files
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Importing from the command-line interface is useful when you want to import files automatically, e.g., once a day at midnight.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface

1. Open the Command Prompt window
2. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
3. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the values of the required arguments: reqifimport project_descriptor=<project URL> reqif_file=<path to ReqIF file> owner=<element hyperlink or qualified name> Where:
  - **project_descriptor** is the URL of a project. How to get a project descriptor?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the main menu, go to **File** > **Project Properties** and select the **General** tab.Find the project descriptor specified in the **Project Descriptor** box at the bottom of the **Project Properties** dialog and copy it to the clipboard.
  - **reqif_file** is the path to the ReqIF file you want to import. If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote(\").
  - **owner** is the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. If newly imported elements are not contained by the Specification element, they are placed under the specified owner. Note that the **owner** argument is optional. How to get the hyperlink of an element?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the Containment tree, right-click an element and select **Copy Element Hyperlink**.The hyperlink of the element is now copied to the clipboard.
4. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

##### Using a properties file

To make importing from the command-line interface quicker you can specify arguments in a properties file, which can be easily edited or reused.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface using a properties file

1. Create a properties file and specify the values of the following arguments: See the example of a properties file below. text1trueProject Properties > General.
#
reqif_file=C:/Users/johnsmith/Documents/project_requirements.reqif
#
# Specify the path to the ReqIF file.
# NOTE: If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote (\").
#
owner=mdel://eee_1045467100313_135436_1
#
# Specify the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. 
# To get the hyperlink of an element, open a project, right-click the element in the Containment tree and select "Copy Element Hyperlink".
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]> When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.
  - project_descriptor
  - reqif_file
  - owner
2. Open the Command Prompt window.
3. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
4. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the **properties_file** argument. reqifimport properties_file=<path to properties file> Where **properties_file** is the path to the properties file to be used.
5. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

reqif_file=&lt;path to ReqIF file&gt; owner=&lt;element hyperlink or qualified name&gt;</pre><p>Where:</p><ul><li><p><strong>project_descriptor</strong> is the URL of a project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc06a5ad-5a37-4ffe-bbdf-b3d946ace80e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to get a project descriptor?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Start your modeling tool and open the required project.</li><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>File</strong> &gt; #

# To get the hyperlink of an element, open a project, right-click the element in the Containment tree and select "Copy Element Hyperlink".
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a747b836-052e-4bff-a358-e5edaed77878"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Open the Command Prompt window.</li><li>Go to the <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;model editor installa</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">tion </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">directory&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements</span></em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> directory containing the <em>reqifimport.exe</em> file.</span></li><li><p>At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the <strong>properties_file</strong> argument.</p><pre>reqifimport properties_file=&lt;path to properties file&gt;</pre><p>Where <strong>properties_file</strong> is the path to the properties file to be used.<br /><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Press <strong>Enter</strong>.</span></span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999500 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00967: (2026x Refresh1) Improving performance

- page_id: `303999500`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999500/2026x+Refresh1+Improving+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:02.549+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:</p><ul><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase memory allocation value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[memory monitor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to track memory usage.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase an active validation period]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.</p></li><li><p>Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Garbage Collector]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to free unused memory.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cancel the background tasks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that run too long.</li><li>Set the limit of open diagrams in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog (<strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagram Tabs Limit</strong>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bee057bf-13ac-4c87-a9c6-d0ef312493f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Disabling inbound network activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990928 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00968: (2026x Refresh1) Include

- page_id: `303990928`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990928/2026x+Refresh1+Include
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:12.994+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Component with the stereotype XSDinclude. This component must be added into an xsd file component.

- annotation maps to the UML Component documentation
- schemaLocation maps to the UML Component name.

#### PANEL: include XML representation summary

include XML representation summary

```text
<include
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
schemaLocation = anyURI
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</include>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### include UML model example.

#### PANEL: include XML code sample

include XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com">	<xs:include schemaLocation = "http://nomagic.com/schema.xsd" /></xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Component with the stereotype XSDinclude. This component must be added into an xsd file component.</p><ul><li>annotation maps to the UML Component documentation</li><li style="text-align: left;">schemaLocation maps to the UML Component name.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6d9ae78-cc28-4cc5-86c8-719fdbca0786"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">include XML representation summary</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;include</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	schemaLocation = anyURI</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/include&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 141 include UML Model Example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Include" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">include UML model example.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="779f4f21-1657-4460-80c3-5d6151cec40c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">include XML code sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:schema xmlns:nm = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot; xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot;&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:include schemaLocation = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com/schema.xsd">http://nomagic.com/schema.xsd</a>&quot; /&gt;<br />&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997945 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00969: (2026x Refresh1) Including external images

- page_id: `303997945`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997945/2026x+Refresh1+Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:47.991+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools > (2026x Refresh1) Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
````html
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998932 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00970: (2026x Refresh1) Inclusive Gateway

- page_id: `303998932`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998932/2026x+Refresh1+Inclusive+Gateway
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:31.799+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Gateways
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485738387

1485738387

1485738387

**Description**

A diverging Inclusive Gateway (Inclusive Decision) is used to create not only alternative but also parallel paths within a process flow. Unlike an Exclusive Gateway, it evaluates all condition expressions. The true evaluation of one condition expression does not exclude the evaluation of the other condition expressions. All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.

Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.

A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diverging Inclusive Gateway

1485738386

**Related elements**

- Gateways
- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.</p><p>Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.</p><p>A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gateway_inclusive.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inclusive Gateway" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diverging_inclusive_gateway.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inclusive Gateway" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Diverging Inclusive Gateway</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca9df4a0-8117-467a-9230-51a9a9d2126f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485738386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Gateways">Gateways</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sequence+Flow">Sequence Flow</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Creating+and+Using+a+Sequence+Flow">Creating and Using a Sequence Flow</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996647 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00971: (2026x Refresh1) Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `303996647`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996647/2026x+Refresh1+Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:12.562+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Refactoring > (2026x Refresh1) Converting Elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Incompatible properties and related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991926 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00972: (2026x Refresh1) Indexing

- page_id: `303991926`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991926/2026x+Refresh1+Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:20.556+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements' space=''] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992752 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00973: (2026x Refresh1) Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `303992752`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992752/2026x+Refresh1+Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:16.455+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning > (2026x Refresh1) Decomposing model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources.  If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.</p><p>A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:</p><p>A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify elements for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="108124cd-4ada-4792-92e5-e3120682817a">indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="77374505-fd83-4671-b796-37de755e139c">Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Indexing</strong> property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_indexing.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Indexing of unloaded projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Common Indexing</strong> option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.</p></li><li><p>When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the <strong>Custom Indexing</strong> option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant.</p><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>) and select the <strong>Use Index</strong> check box. </p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d3bdc33-2e2d-4238-8f19-7a0e4fd3b85c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487609551

1487609552

1487609552

**Description**

An Influencing Organization is an organization that is external to the enterprise modeled in a given enterprise BMM, and that influences that enterprise.

An Influencing Organization is the source of Influencer. The Influencer may have multiple sources, or none.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487609550

**Related elements**

- Influencer Concepts
- Influence Concepts Relationship

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993619 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00978: (2026x Refresh1) Inheritance tab

- page_id: `303993619`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993619/2026x+Refresh1+Inheritance+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:22.460+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425598369

1425598371

1425598370

INLINE

The Inheritance tab represents classifiers (e.g., packages, data types, and stereotypes) hierarchy within your project. Inheritance according to the UML Specification is shown using the generalization relationship.

If a model element has no generalization relationship, it will not be represented in the Inheritance tab.

To open a new Inheritance tree

1. Open the Containment , Structure , or Inheritance tab.
2. In the open tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_new_tab_button.png'] and then select Open in New Inheritance Tree .

You can change some Inheritance tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Inheritance tab consists of the following two components:

- Inheritance tab toolbar
- Inheritance tree

##### Inheritance tab toolbar

The Inheritance tab toolbar contains the following buttons

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Invert Tree - inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the childelement at the top of the hierarchy.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree. |

##### Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree

You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Structure Tree | Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML v1). |

1425598368

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space='']

The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.</p><p>You can choose to open elements in:</p><ul><li>New Containment Tree</li><li>New Structure Tree</li><li>New Inheritance Tree</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_tab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites<span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span></li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Options</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Options menu and then select:</p><ul><li><strong><strong>Invert Tree</strong> </strong>- inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the child<br />element at the top of the hierarchy.<strong><br /></strong></li><li><strong>Show </strong><strong>Applied Stereotypes</strong> - shows or hides the applied <br />stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree</h3><p><br /></p><p>You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 39.6427%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.0677%;" /><col style="width: 71.9323%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Select in Structure Tree</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af49f33a-5b79-474a-834d-d8ee5e6a182b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML v1).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b778001b-c783-474f-92d7-aaa6e02f945c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425598368</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000422 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00979: (2026x Refresh1) Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `304000422`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000422/2026x+Refresh1+Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:08.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.

The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:

###### Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges

Let's say, we have Operational Performers *Helicopter*and *Signal Receiver* related to the Operational Exchange *Reported Condition*. Then we draw an association between *Medical Assistance* and *Signal Receiver*. In this case, the drawn association can realize the *Reported Condition* exchange, since the Operational Performer *Medical Assistance* aggregates the Operational Performer *Helicopter*.

If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.

All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the aggregated exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Aggregated Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Case 2. Inherited Exchanges

Let's say, we have an Operational Performers *Search**Node* and *Place Of Safety* related to the Operational Exchange *Warning Order*. The Operational Performer *Aircraft* specifies the Operational Performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place O**f**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991443 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00980: (2026x Refresh1) Initial Values

- page_id: `303991443`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991443/2026x+Refresh1+Initial+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:43.421+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['initial-values', 'context-specific-values', 'default-values', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1345490194

1345490196

1345490195

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] with a label of *initial values* is used to show values of properties belonging to a containing block. These values override any default values that may have been previously specified on these properties on their originally defining block. Initial value compartments may be specified within nested properties, which then apply only in the particular usage context defined by the outermost containing block.

The purpose of initial value compartments is to show various values as a result of a reconfigured selected context. Initial value compartments allows to:

- Create different configurations for the same structure and display them directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] .
- Have different values for the same part in different contexts.
- Assign a different initial value to an inherited property.

The *initial value*s compartment can be shown on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape compartment and display the values of the properties reconfigured in a selected context.

The procedures below describes how to work with initial values and its compartments:

43

##### Defining initial values

You can define context-specific initial values by overriding any default values that was previously specified on Value Properties on their originally defining Block. Before defining a context-specific initial value, the followingprerequisites must be met:

- The Value Property must be created for a Block which is a type of Part Property.
- The Type and Default Value are defined for the Value Property in its Specification window.

To defineinitial value

1. Right-click the Part Property shape with default value defined.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > **Define Context-Specific Initial Value**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. From the **Select Property** dialog, select Value Property which you want to redefine. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] After selecting the Value Property, the structure of instances is created automatically in the Model Browser.
4. Type the value directly on the Part Property shape. If you can not specify the new value, check if the context of that Part Property is correct. ]]>****
5. Press Enter. The slot with newly defined value is created for the Instance Specification. The default value is not changed. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying/hiding *initial values* compartments

You can display/hide initial values on the Part Property Compartments. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 specific compartments' space=''].

To show/hide Initial Values on Part Property compartment, do one of the following

- Select the Part Property shape, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and select Initial Values. [ATTACHMENT filename='initial_value_compartments.png']
- Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode, and set the Suppress Context Specific Values property value to true to show initial values, or false - to hide them.

##### contextSelecting the context of initial values

Initial values which are shown in the Part Property compartments depends from the selected context. For example, if the selected context is A then the compartment show values of A.

To select a context of initial values

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the All mode.
3. Select the Context of Context-Specific Values property value box, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_value_button_in_specification_window.png'] .
4. From the Select Element dialog, choose the element which will be the context.
5. Click OK .
6. Click Close . The context for initial values is specified.

##### Displaying/hiding element type in the *initial values* compartments

You can display or hide the elements types in the *initial values* compartments of a part using either the Symbol(s) Properties dialog or the part shortcut menu.

To display/hide element type in the *initial values* compartments

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and select the Symbol Properties command.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode.
3. Select the Show Slot Type property value from drop-down list:

- 
  - None - to hide types.
  - Name - to display names of the element types.
  - Qualified Name - to display qualified names of the element types.

1345490192

**Related pages**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Phone Camera** sample model. To open this sample download [ATTACHMENT filename='PhoneCamerasInitialValues.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47d19cb7-47f1-4b00-93b4-d9b22d2a457b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490194</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1eb25bb-910e-4f04-b6dd-063e66529a5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490196</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9fd3ad-d5f3-47ca-87fd-c98ba8dbf1a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490195</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with a label of <em>initial values</em> is used to show values of properties belonging to a containing block. These values override any default values that may have been previously specified on these properties on their originally defining block. Initial value compartments may be specified within nested properties, which then apply only in the particular usage context defined by the outermost containing block.</p><p>The purpose of initial value compartments is to show various values as a result of a reconfigured selected context. ac:macro-id="1390d4bb-dd62-455b-abe4-5f48a6963948"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Defining initial
````
