rgb(51,51,51);">context-specific</span></span> initial values by overriding any default values that was previously specified on Value Properties on their originally defining Block. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Before d<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">efining a context-specific initial value</span></span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the following </span></span></span>prerequisites must be met:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Value Property must be created for a Block which is a type of Part Property.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Type and Default Value are defined for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Value Property</span> in its Specification window.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(51,51,51);" /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To define<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> initial value</span></span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape with default value defined.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Define Context-Specific Initial Value</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="define_context_specific_initial_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the <strong>Select Property</strong> dialog, select Value Property which you want to redefine.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_value_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="859840e1-2833-42ff-9ee9-5ad663573c04"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the Value Property, the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">structure of instances is created automatically in the Model Browser</span>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Type the value directly on the Part Property shape.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b4c080f-31ca-4155-96c2-47d992684b8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not specify the new value<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, check if the context of that Part Property is correct. <ac:link ac:anchor="context"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select the context of initial value >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><strong> </strong></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Press Enter.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span>The slot with newly defined value is created for the Instance Specification. The default value is not changed.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="defined_initial_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><h3>Displaying/hiding <em>initial values</em> compartments</h3><p>You can display/hide initial values on the Part Property Compartments. Read more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 specific compartments" /></ac:link>.</p><p>To show/hide Initial Values on Part Property compartment, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the Part Property shape, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and select Initial Values.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_value_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties. In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Expert</strong> or <strong>All</strong> mode, and set the <strong>Suppress Context Specific Values</strong> property value to true to show initial values, or false - to hide them.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e05be940-64f5-4589-a6ba-a9ee129722b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">context</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Selecting the context of initial values</h3><p>Initial values which are shown in the Part Property compartments depends from the selected context. For example, if the selected context is A then the compartment show values of A.</p><p>To select a context of initial values</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li>Select the <strong>Context of Context-Specific Values</strong> property value box, and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, choose the element which will be the context.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The context for initial values is specified.</li></ol><h3>Displaying/hiding element type in the <em>initial values</em> compartments</h3><p>You can display or hide the elements types in the <em>initial values</em> compartments of a part using either the Symbol(s) Properties dialog or the part shortcut menu.</p><p>To display/hide element type in the <em>initial values</em> compartments</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and select the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> command.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Expert</strong> or <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li>Select the <strong>Show Slot Type</strong> property value from drop-down list:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>None</strong> - to hide types.</li><li><strong>Name</strong> - to display names of the element types.</li><li><strong>Qualified Name</strong> - to display qualified names of the element types.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70fa2bb3-0cc2-4788-8fe3-6c09aa836f15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490192</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="08d7c3dc-6584-461a-8cf3-c0be512dea3e" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Phone Camera</strong> sample model. To open this sample download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="PhoneCamerasInitialValues.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994580 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00981: (2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path

- page_id: `303994580`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994580/2026x+Refresh1+Inserting+a+shape+on+the+path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:25.652+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['split-transition', 'insert-shape-on-path', 'split-path', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space='']StateMachine and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''] diagrams.

In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. 
 
To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane

1. Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.
2. Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.
3. In the **Split Transition** dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:

- **Before Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn. For example, *Password read* transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to*Verification* State. If you want to insert the*Read password* State before the *Password read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **Before Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **After Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn. For example, *Name**read* Transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to *Verification* State. If you want to insert the *Read**password* State after the *Name read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **After Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Do Not Insert**button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.

In the **Split Transition** dialog, select the **Remember my choice** check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.

****

This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the <strong>Split Transition</strong> dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Before Transition </strong>button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22b0b0c-fd35-4399-84a2-38e5a213de0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Password read</em> transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to<em> Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the<em> Read password</em> State before the <em>Password read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>Before Transition</strong> button.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="200" ac:width="160"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="242" ac:width="163"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_before_path_with_state.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>After <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Transition </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button</span>. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125c74a4-a702-4fa7-af1e-ad6533f7bf0f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Name </em><em>read</em> Transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to <em>Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the <em>Read</em><em> password</em> State after the <em>Name read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>After Transition</strong> button. </span></p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="222" ac:width="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="252" ac:width="154"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_after_path_with_state.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Do Not Insert </strong>button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69da0fcd-52d9-42db-9ab9-6f589edb6390"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog</span>, select the <strong>Remember my choice</strong> check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Split Transition dialog" ac:alt="Split Transition dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="split_transition_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /></strong></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997333 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00982: (2026x Refresh1) Inserting images into HTML text

- page_id: `303997333`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997333/2026x+Refresh1+Inserting+images+into+HTML+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:03.597+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) HTML editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

INLINE

To insert an image into HTML text

#### EXPAND: From the Containment tree

From the Containment tree

- When the image is already an AttachedFile, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with attached files' space=''] .

#### EXPAND: By using the Advanced HTML Editor

By using the Advanced HTML Editor

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space=''] .
2. On the toolbar, click Insert Image button [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] , then click one of the following:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] From File – to browse the image in your file system or paste the attached image file URL into the Picture source field.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_attached_image.png'] From Attached File – to open the Select Elements dialog and select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **From Image Library** – to use images from a bundled image collection or your own added sets of images.
3. Click OK twice to close both dialogs.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

1. Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the **Paste Special**menu, select **Image**to paste image and attach it to the project or **Image (without attaching)** to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the **Select Image Owner**, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click **Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click**OK**to close the dialogs. If you chose to not attach the image, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device.This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size. This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste image and attach it to the project or HTML Text to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on the HTML Editor. This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select HTML Text to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select Image to paste and attach it to the project, or select Plain Text to paste only text without an image.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click**Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on HTML Editor. Only rich text from MS Office files is supported. If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.

To insert multiple image files

1. Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, then do either:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste images and attach them to the project or Image (without attaching) to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.
2. Click OK to close the dialogs.

This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:macro-id="4fbf7bcd-d9c1-4ef4-b44f-4c9a549079c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging the image file from file system</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste <span>them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste image and attach it to the project or <strong>Image (without attaching)</strong> to paste it <span>only for referencing in the diagram.</span></span></li><li><span><span>drag them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Select Image Owner</strong>, select <span>the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.<br /></span></span></span><span> </span></li></ul></li><li><p><span>Click </span><strong>OK</strong><span> to close the dialogs.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b646714-975a-4f24-a3d6-4e0cf5e55428"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you chose to not attach the image</span><span>, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device. </span>This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47f1a584-94e6-4a50-a47c-7934abbd0568"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="145a8115-0b56-4910-b30f-a0c0c178fb48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste image and attach it to the project or <strong>HTML Text </strong>to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.</li><li>drag the image to the HTML Editor. <br /> </li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialogs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fecd06b5-48f2-45a1-8f1a-c89fe4fd751f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only on the HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7c7466d-6f09-4cfa-a546-ea83bdc9c375"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a98311b7-6cb5-4786-8159-e7ee54a5b5c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging rich text with an image</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>HTML Text </strong><span>to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select </span><strong>Image </strong>to paste and attach it to the project, or select <strong>Plain Text </strong>to paste only text without an image.</li><li>drag the image to the HTML Editor. <span>In the </span><strong>Select Image Owner</strong><span>, select </span><span>the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. <span>If you click </span><strong>Cancel</strong><span>, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.</span></span><br /> </li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialogs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b066056-1c53-491b-a8aa-79fee0ae44c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only on HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="129a3559-d8b8-4743-8106-3d12af578491"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only rich text from MS Office files is supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc5590d-0202-4fb4-bfa2-630e7250d04d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To insert multiple image files</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, <span>then do either:<br /> <br /></span><ul><li>copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste images and attach them to the project or <strong>Image (without attaching)</strong> to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.</li><li>drag them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Select Image Owner</strong>, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.<br /> </li></ul></li><li><span>Click </span><strong>OK</strong><span> to close the dialogs.</span><br /><span><span> </span></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6bc8859-213d-46a9-a4ce-ecb246d51a95"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996760 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00983: (2026x Refresh1) Inspecting changes in the Change details panel

- page_id: `303996760`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996760/2026x+Refresh1+Inspecting+changes+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:19.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['change-details', 'merge-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991256206

INLINE

1991256208

INLINE

1991256207

INLINE

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the changes that occurred either on the element selected in the **Merged** **Result** tree/list or on the property selected in the **Specification** panel. The panel is located under the **Specification** panel on the **Merge** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge. In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding change concept' space=''].

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed to numerical/text types accordingly.

1991256204

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992086 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00984: (2026x Refresh1) Inspecting differences in the Change details panel

- page_id: `303992086`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992086/2026x+Refresh1+Inspecting+differences+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:29.825+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Comparing projects > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['change-details', 'differences', 'difference-viewer', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the **Differences** tree or the property selected in the **Specification** panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the **Change** **details** panel. The panel is located above the **Specification** panel on the **Difference Viewer** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch – Changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding change types' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Change details</strong> panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree or the property selected in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the <strong>Change</strong> <strong>details</strong> panel. The panel is located above the <strong>Specification</strong> panel on the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window.</p><p>The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edfa489e-d703-4e81-84d4-4e561ff6619a">– Changes.</ac:inline-comment-marker>   <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Change details dialog" ac:alt="Change details dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_details_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting differences in the Change details panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the <strong>Dependent changes</strong> branch under an element with dependent changes.</p><p>For the dependent changes description, r<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="319a5311-84d4-4531-8f99-20cc6ed2e97e">efer t</ac:inline-comment-marker>o <ac:link ac:anchor="dependentchange"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependent changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992066 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00985: (2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel

- page_id: `303992066`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992066/2026x+Refresh1+Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+differences+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:28.322+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Comparing projects > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['property-changes', 'compare-specification-panel', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer** window. All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in the **Differences** tree.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both base and compared projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Differences** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Differences tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- I n the Base or Compared column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 **Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post- conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are appropriately highlighted. As of version 18.3, the differences between compared text written in HTML are now represented as rich text, enabling you to see all changes in formatting as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can see all the details of the element property changes in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel that is located at the bottom of the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window. All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869">the </ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869">Differences</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869"> tree.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes t<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="027b7e7d-5dca-4150-815d-e43fed998a3a">hat have been d</ac:inline-comment-marker>etected in both base and compared projects.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Displaying element property changes in Specification panel" ac:title="Displaying element property changes in Specification panel"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_specification_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To navigate from the <strong>Specification</strong> panel to the <strong>Differences</strong> tree, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Properties</strong> column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Differences tree" ac:title="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Differences tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_specification_panel_shortcut.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>Inspecting differences between textual values</strong> </span></p><p>You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post- conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are appropriately highlighted. As of version 18.3, the differences between compared text written in HTML are now represented as rich text, enabling you to see all changes in formatting as well.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Displaying differences between long textual property values" ac:title="Displaying differences between long textual property values"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6cb413be-44fc-4f1b-81ae-6764a0ebb8c2">To see the differences of compared texts, do either</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li>Select a property with a modified text on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_values_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Double-click the property on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. The <strong>Compare p<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d112cebf-7f31-45f8-a49d-efa7ed5fd504">roperty</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d112cebf-7f31-45f8-a49d-efa7ed5fd504"> dialog opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996762 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00986: (2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel

- page_id: `303996762`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996762/2026x+Refresh1+Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:20.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-specification-panel', 'property-changes', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1086956613

1086956615

1086956614

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the top right of the **Merge** window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the **Merged Result** tree or list.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Merged Result** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Merged Result Tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- In the Source , Ancestor , Target or Merge Result Preview column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Ancestor** column by default is hidden. To show it in the **Specification**panel, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the **Merge**toolbar and select **Show Ancestor**.

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.

**Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

1086956612

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Merge' space='']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42c73278-e0ac-4af9-baf4-f60c5d8d41a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956613</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53b90e4c-5e54-41f0-bfc8-eb338435272c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7acdc9f-ea28-4f36-b426-b6e0b0d3ef53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956614</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="527afc11-f044-4d81-8354-777b9b3658dc">You</ac:inline-comment-marker> can see all the details of the element property changes in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel that is located at the top right of the <strong>Merge</strong> window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the <strong>Merged Result</strong> tree or list. </p><p>As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Speification_pnel.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To navigate from the <strong>Specification</strong> panel to the <strong>Merged Result</strong> tree, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Properties</strong> column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to <strong>Select in Merged Result Tree</strong> and choose an element to which you want to navigate.</li><li>In the <strong>Source</strong>, <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8003c2bf-ac4a-4600-9ea2-8cf0c433339a">Ancestor</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong>, <strong>Target</strong>  or <strong>Merge Result Preview </strong>column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Merged Result tree" ac:alt="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Merged Result tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_specification_panel_shortcut.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d0e411c-a637-4371-bad1-75f3d1034917"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Ancestor</strong> column by default is hidden. To show it in the <strong>Specification </strong>panel, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the <strong>Merge </strong>toolbar and select <strong>Show Ancestor</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf7040c7-d539-4d2e-9200-a5fe8257dafa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Inspecting differences between textual values</strong></span></p><p>You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Displaying differences between long textual property values" ac:alt="Displaying differences between long textual property values"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To see the differences of compared texts, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a property with a modified text on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_values_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Double-click the property on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. The <strong>Compare property</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6377b1e-13f8-49c1-a178-f4b81f42ac6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956612</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0cd9eacb-fae7-4d1c-a555-09f2ddeb0192"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Merge" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998404 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00987: (2026x Refresh1) Installation

- page_id: `303998404`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998404/2026x+Refresh1+Installation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:39.162+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard template editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word

1. Copy all files and subdirectories from the <install.dir>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba folder to %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP**** (%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,
  - On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows Vista: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows 7, 8, and later: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
2. Restart Microsoft Word.

Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:

1. Go to [https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019](https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019)

2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls

3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy all files and subdirectories from the <em>&lt;install.dir&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba</em> folder to <em>%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP<strong> </strong></em>(%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,<br /><ul><li>On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: <em>C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows Vista: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows 7, 8, and later: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li></ul></li><li>Restart Microsoft Word.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edbea50e-de80-4591-a358-d537c22b71e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:</p><p>1. Go to <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019">https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019</a></p><p>2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls</p><p>3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990558 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00989: (2026x Refresh1) Installing custom tool

- page_id: `303990558`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990558/2026x+Refresh1+Installing+custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:46.693+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install the extension, copy the *JAR* file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new *JAR* file when a new report is generated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install the extension, copy the <em>JAR</em> file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new <em>JAR</em> file when a new report is generated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991747 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00990: (2026x Refresh1) Instance Specifications export to the Modelica file

- page_id: `303991747`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991747/2026x+Refresh1+Instance+Specifications+export+to+the+Modelica+file
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:17.148+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export to the Modelica file a particular configuration with initial values and subtype instances as specified in the instance model. The mechanism generates Modelica (.mo) file with an Instance Specification name and save it to your file directory. In the following figure, the *p* Instance Specification is exported to the Modelica file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The export of*p* Instance Specification to the Modelica file.

****

To export a configuration with initial values

1. In the Model Browser , select an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] you want to export and do one of the following:
  - In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Export To > Modelica .
  - Right-click the selected Instance Specification and then click Tools > Export to Modelica .
2. Specify export options in the Modelica Export Options dialog. Option descriptions >>
3. Click OK . The Modelica (.mo) file is generated and saved to your file directory.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can export to the Modelica file a particular configuration with initial values and subtype instances as specified in the instance model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The mechanism generates Modelica (.mo) file with an Instance Specification name and save it to your file directory. In the following figure, the <em>p</em> Instance Specification is exported to the Modelica file.</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_export_to_modelica.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Instance Specifications export to the Modelica file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The export of<em> p</em> Instance Specification to the Modelica file.<br /></span></h6><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To export a configuration with initial values</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="610b09e8-ae83-4af6-ba38-6359e1ae4370">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Model+Browser">Model Browser</a>, select an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link> you want to export and do one of the following:<ul><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Export To &gt; Modelica</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the selected Instance Specification and then click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Export to Modelica</strong>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="74adf39c-1abc-4872-a7c0-afc0769e6f2e">Specify export options in the <strong>Modelica Export Options</strong> dialog. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Modelica+export#Modelicaexport-ModelicaExportOptions" rel="nofollow">Option descriptions &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="4aef65ad-de83-481c-9e79-241458d6be63">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The Modelica (.mo) file is generated and saved to your file directory.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994323 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00991: (2026x Refresh1) Instance table

- page_id: `303994323`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994323/2026x+Refresh1+Instance+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:09.035+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

221798943

221798946

221798941

**On this page**

221798945

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Instance Table is one of four tables which allow you to manage Instance Specifications of your model in a spreadsheet-like form. Each row in the table represents an instance.****

##### **Purpose**

An Instance Table allows you to:

- Create Instance Specifications (instances) for the classifiers.
- Review the instances of one or more of the classifiers in a single place.
- Edit Slot values of the instances displayed in the table.
- Customize the representation of the table.
- Export the data into .html , .csv, or * .xlsx files.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Instance Table**

The following outlines the specific procedures when working with the Instance Table:

221837881

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric table' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000190 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00992: (2026x Refresh1) Instantiating structures

- page_id: `304000190`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000190/2026x+Refresh1+Instantiating+structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:53.699+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'instant-structures', 'typical-uaf-element', 'actual-uaf-element', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard opens.

To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4.png']
2. Right-click them and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UAF element | Actual UAF element |
| --- | --- |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |
| Project Milestone Role | Actual Project Milestone Role |
| Organization | Actual Organization |
| Sub Organization (Resource Role typed by and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Post | Actual Post |
| Post Role (Resource Role typed by Post and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Person | Actual Person |
| Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set |
| Measurement | Actual Measurement |
| Location | Actual Location |
| Capability Configuration | Fielded Capability |
| Environment | Actual Environment |
| Condition | Actual Condition |
| Resource Performer | Actual Resource |
| Enterprise Phase | Actual Enterprise Phase |
| Enduring Task | Actual Enduring Task |
| Responsibility | Actual Responsibility |
| Risk | Actual Risk |
| Natural Resource | Actual Resource |
| Resource Architecture | Actual Resource |
| Resource Artifact | Actual Resource |
| Software | Actual Resource |
| Technology | Actual Resource |
| System | Actual Resource |
| Resource Mitigation | Actual Resource |
| Security Enclave | Actual Resource |
| Known Resource | Actual Resource |
| Operational Performer | Actual Property Set |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.</li><li>Right-click them and choose <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong>.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To instantiate the proposed typical structure</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.</p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Typical" ac:alt="OV-4 Typical"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Instantiating structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Right-click them and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong><strong> Instance</strong>.<span> </span></p></li><li><p>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> Actual diagram is created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Instantiating structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.</p><p> </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Typical UAF element</strong></p></td><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Actual</strong> <strong>UAF</strong> <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000188 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00994: (2026x Refresh1) Integration with the Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `304000188`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000188/2026x+Refresh1+Integration+with+the+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:53.597+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin. 
You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the **Concept Modeling Diagram** from the **Create Diagram** dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.

For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeler Documentation' space='MCM'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin.<br />You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the <strong>Concept Modeling Diagram</strong> from the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.</p><p>For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM" ri:content-title="Concept Modeler Documentation" /></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994027 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00995: (2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram

- page_id: `303994027`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994027/2026x+Refresh1+Interaction+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:49.867+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308856586

308856588

308856587

**Overview**

An Interaction Overview diagram is a type of Interaction diagram, which falls under the behavioral diagramming family.An Interaction Overview diagram focuses on the flow of control between interactions. It is based on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''] notation. Interactions in the Interaction Overview diagram are represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED'] element. All elements in the diagram, except for the Interaction Use, are the same as in the Activity diagram.

**Purpose**

An Interaction Overview diagram is intended to be used to model the flow of logic in a series of interactions. To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities).

**Usage**

Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram.

**Summary**

Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:

- provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions
- keep the focus on the "big picture" and away from implementation details
- encourage the reuse of interactions

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Interaction Overview diagram

308856585

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

It is based on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram" /></ac:link> notation. Interactions in the Interaction Overview diagram are represented using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Interaction Use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> element. All elements in the diagram, except for the Interaction Use, are the same as in the Activity diagram.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>An Interaction Overview diagram is intended to be used to model the flow of logic in a series of interactions. To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities). </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram. </p><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions</li><li>keep the focus on the &quot;big picture&quot; and away from implementation details</li><li>encourage the reuse of interactions </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interaction_overview_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of an Interaction Overview diagram<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2a23b37-52a4-4422-9417-7709aa16f0f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308856585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997006 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00996: (2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram elements

- page_id: `303997006`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997006/2026x+Refresh1+Interaction+Overview+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:41.616+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x Refresh1) Enterprise Architect Import > (2026x Refresh1) Special Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

560251696

560251698

560251694

**On this page**

3

560251697

The following sections describe additional EA specific Interaction Overview diagram information.

##### Interaction Overview Diagram

An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram' space=''] is one of four types of Interaction diagrams. The other three are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Time diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Communication diagram' space='']diagrams. An Interaction Overview diagram, just like an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''], visualizes a sequence of activities. Most of the notation elements for Interaction Overview diagrams are the same as those for Activity diagrams (such as Initial, Decision, Fork, Join, and Final [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MED']). Interaction Occurrences and Interaction elements are two new elements in the interaction overview diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram.

Afterconversion, the Interaction Overview diagram will be placed within anActivityelement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram Placement.

###### Interaction Element

Interaction elements display an inline Interaction diagram (Interaction Overview, Timing, Sequence, or Communication diagram). In EA, Interaction elements can be created to display other diagrams than those classified as Interaction diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Element in Interaction Overview Diagram.

###### Interaction Occurrence

Interaction Occurrence elements refer to an existing Interaction diagram. They are visually represented by a rectangular frame, which can be created by dragging the Interaction diagram from the project Browser to an Interaction Overview diagram and selecting the **Interaction Occurrence** command from the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Occurence element.

560258099

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram' space='']

Interaction Occurrences and Interaction elements are two new elements in the interaction overview diagrams.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interactionOverview.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Interaction Overview Diagram.</h6><p><span>After<span> </span>conversion, the Interaction Overview diagram will be placed within an<span> </span>Activity<span> </span>element.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interactionOverview_placement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Interaction Overview Diagram Placement.</h6><h4>Interaction Element</h4><p>Interaction elements display an inline Interaction diagram (Interaction Overview, Timing, Sequence, or Communication diagram). In EA, Interaction elements can be created to display other diagrams than those classified as Interaction diagrams.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interactionElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Interaction element.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interactionElement_inOverview.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Interaction Element in Interaction Overview Diagram.</h6><h4>Interaction Occurrence</h4><p>Interaction Occurrence elements refer to an existing Interaction diagram. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993803 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00997: (2026x Refresh1) Interchanging of requirements using Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `303993803`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993803/2026x+Refresh1+Interchanging+of+requirements+using+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:06.829+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Importing requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Cameo DataHub is a dedicated solution for the requirements synchronization between different sources, such as MagicDraw, Rational DOORS®, CSV files, and other. Cameo DataHub is a data bridge that allows for data importing and exporting, automatic synchronization and creation of references between requirements and other artifacts. It allows to use different import and synchronization methods while importing or exporting requirements.

Came DataHub allows to:

- Associate and synchronize data.
- Change the monitoring statuses such as active, pending update, and pending delete whenever associated data items are modified or deleted.
- Impact monitoring. Whenever the referenced data item changes, Cameo DataHub reports the suspect status to direct traceability from each requirement to the respective design artifact.
- Define mappings between data and its’ properties.

The Cameo DataHub must be installed before taking the following actions. For more information refer to, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='CDH'].

To interchange of requirements using Cameo DataHub

1. Open an existing project or create a new one.
2. On the main menu, click Tools > Requirements > DataHub and select a desired action.

For more information about working with Cameo DataHub, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='CDH'].

800700

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='CDH']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='CDH']
- Importing requirements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

