Whenever the referenced data item changes, Cameo DataHub reports the suspect status to direct traceability from each requirement to the respective design artifact.</li><li>Define mappings between data and its’ properties.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="032c5bb3-f0a2-4ffd-b155-7e6e92f7f095"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Cameo DataHub must be installed before taking the following actions. For more information refer to, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Installation, licensing, and system requirements" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To interchange of requirements using Cameo DataHub</p><hr /><ol><li>Open an existing project or create a new one.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Requirements</strong> &gt; <strong>DataHub</strong> and select a desired action.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28a8a749-ad13-40eb-9a49-b30d3085cc71"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with Cameo DataHub, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="User Guide" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo DataHub documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9b1f94a-adc7-45d6-86a7-a2346aa32989"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w12dprJkT_0&amp;t=1s" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">700</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Installation, licensing, and system requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing Cameo DataHub]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="User Guide" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo DataHub documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Importing+requirements">Importing requirements</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990532 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00998: (2026x Refresh1) Intermediate Catch Event

- page_id: `303990532`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990532/2026x+Refresh1+Intermediate+Catch+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:43.045+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Intermediate Catch Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.

You can use Intermediate Catch Event to:

- Show where messages are expected or sent within a process.
- Show delays that are expected within a process.
- Interrupt normal flow through exception handling.

Types of Intermediate Catch Events are the following:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Intermediate Catch Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.</p><p>You can use Intermediate Catch Event to:</p><ul><li>Show where messages are expected or sent within a process.</li><li>Show delays that are expected within a process.</li><li>Interrupt normal flow through exception handling.</li></ul><p>Types of Intermediate Catch Events are the following:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3979d68d-a16d-4b70-82c8-54bf334f0d8b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

An Intermediate Throwing Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.

You can use Intermediate Throwing Events to show extra work required.

Types of Intermediate Throwing Events are as follows:

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new internal diagram for the element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space='']), the new context element is created automatically.

This feature is available for all UAF diagrams based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space=''].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure Diagram context' space='']true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991906 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01001: (2026x Refresh1) Internal Influencer

- page_id: `303991906`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991906/2026x+Refresh1+Internal+Influencer
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:01.674+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Influencer Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487581411

1487581411

1487581411

**Description**

Internal Influencer is an enterprise that can impact its employment of Means or achievement of Ends.

Internal Influencer is usually categorized the following:

- Assumption: Something that is taken for granted or without proof.
- Explicit Corporate Value: An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with that is explicitly set forth and declared.
- Implicit Corporate Value: A corporate value that is not explicitly declared but nonetheless understood by some or all of the people in an enterprise.
- Habit: A customary practice or use.
- Infrastructure: The basic underlying framework or features of a system.
- Issue: A point in question or a matter that is in dispute as between contending partners.
- Management Prerogative: A right or privilege exercised by virtue of ownership or position in an enterprise.
- Resource: The resources available for carrying out the business of an enterprise, especially their quality.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487581410

**Related elements**

- Influencer Concepts
- Influence Concepts Relationship

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998672 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01002: (2026x Refresh1) Introducing Macro Engine

- page_id: `303998672`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998672/2026x+Refresh1+Introducing+Macro+Engine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:52.975+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Macro Engine (previously called Script Engine) allows you to create your own macro (script) by using BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, or Jython. With Macro Engine, you can control everything that is allowed in Open API, for example, transforming and manipulating models. Learn more about Open API in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG'] and find sample macros in *<modeling tool installation directory>/samples/product features/macros*.

Macro Engine comes with Professional, Architect, and Enterprise Editions starting from version 16.6 and later.

##### Changing Javascript Nashorn to Javascript Rhino

The Macro Engine supports Javascript Rhino. If you have any macros written in Javascript Nashorn, which is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space=''], you can change it to Javascript Rhino.

To change to Javascript Rhino

- Click Tools > Macros > Change JS Nashorn to Rhino

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Macro Engine (previously called Script Engine) allows you to create your own macro (script) by using BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, or Jython. With Macro Engine, you can control everything that is allowed in Open API, for example, transforming and manipulating models. Learn more about Open API in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Developer Guides Home" ri:space-key="DEVG" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Developer Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and find sample macros in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/samples/product features/macros</em>.</p><p>Macro Engine comes with Professional, Architect, and Enterprise Editions starting from version 16.6 and later.</p><h3>Changing Javascript Nashorn to Javascript Rhino</h3><p>The Macro Engine supports Javascript Rhino. If you have any macros written in Javascript Nashorn, which is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[deprecated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can change it to Javascript Rhino.</p><p>To change to Javascript Rhino</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Change JS Nashorn to Rhino</strong></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990453 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01004: (2026x Refresh1) Introduction

- page_id: `303990453`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990453/2026x+Refresh1+Introduction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:32.119+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355068225

1355068226

1355068226

This user guide demonstrates how to create a report template using Report Wizard and use it in the modeling tool. To make the most of Report Wizard, you need to understand Apache Velocity and the modeling tool elements. Report Wizard uses Apache Velocity, this means that you will need to know how to create a Velocity template and that every rule that applies to Velocity will also apply to the Report Wizard.

Velocity is a Java-based template engine that processes templates and references Java objects to produce output documents. A basic Velocity template can contain static text, layouts, conditional statements, and placeholders for each referenced Java object. When a template is being read by Velocity, conditional statements will be processed and placeholders will be replaced with the value from the referenced Java objects. You can use Velocity, for example, to generate web pages, emails, and read *XML* documents, but with Report Wizard it is now possible to use Velocity to read other types of templates, such as Rich Text Format documents. This user guide covers some basic aspects of Velocity, modeling tool elements, and Report Wizard directives.

1355068224

**Related page**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5c469b-c11c-4cff-be12-1bba3a457112"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355068225</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ab531a4-acde-4318-b5c0-529b8d53f6b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355068226</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a32d2eb-d69f-4be1-97c8-bafb80e2249b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355068226</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This user guide demonstrates how to create a report template using Report Wizard and use it in the modeling tool. To make the most of Report Wizard, you need to understand Apache Velocity and the modeling tool elements. Report Wizard uses Apache Velocity, this means that you will need to know how to create a Velocity template and that every rule that applies to Velocity will also apply to the Report Wizard.</p><p>Velocity is a Java-based template engine that processes templates and references Java objects to produce output documents. A basic Velocity template can contain static text, layouts, conditional statements, and placeholders for each referenced Java object. When a template is being read by Velocity, conditional statements will be processed and placeholders will be replaced with the value from the referenced Java objects. You can use Velocity, for example, to generate web pages, emails, and read <em>XML</em> documents, but with Report Wizard it is now possible to use Velocity to read other types of templates, such as Rich Text Format documents. This user guide covers some basic aspects of Velocity, modeling tool elements, and Report Wizard directives.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2794887a-6f98-48f2-9b85-a3f47c2851b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355068224</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4dc280e8-5e54-4745-a5b5-4e96822291c6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The warning message, see the figure below, will open whenever a template contains any invalid syntax, such as using **#forrow** without **#endrow**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An invalid syntax warning message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The warning message, see the figure below, will open whenever a template contains any invalid syntax, such as using <strong>#forrow</strong> without <strong>#endrow</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Invalid Syntax Warning Message" ac:alt="Invalid Syntax Warning Message"><ri:attachment ri:filename="invalid_syntax.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Invalid syntax" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">An invalid syntax warning message.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991157 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01009: (2026x Refresh1) Issues related to collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `303991157`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991157/2026x+Refresh1+Issues+related+to+collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:24.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:

- Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3 is not available.
- The Change Library Version action is unavailable for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using libraries' space='']
- Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.

For more information about working with the platform, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3 is not available.</li><li>The <strong>Change Library Version</strong> action is unavailable for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UJTL and DISR libraries.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7e78b6-ea0a-4e98-bb73-e0ed5c2cbe1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the platform, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989743 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01010: (2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management

- page_id: `303989743`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989743/2026x+Refresh1+Item+Flow+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:17:55.015+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create Item Flows in two modes:

- Direct
- Between Part Types

The *Direct* creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

The *Between Part Types* creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between Part Property types. In this mode, you can quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages.

To choose the creation mode

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The Project Option dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group and select SysML .
3. From the Item Flow Creation Mode option, choose one of the following:
  - Direct
  - Between Part Types
4. Click OK when you are done.

For more information on working with Item Flows, see the following chapters:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create Item Flows in two modes:</p><ul><li>Direct</li><li>Between Part Types</li></ul><p>The <em>Direct</em> creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>The <em>Between Part Types</em> creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between Part Property types. In this mode, you can quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages.</p><p><br /></p><p>To choose the creation mode</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Option</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Expand the <strong>General</strong> options group and select <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>From the<strong> Item Flow Creation Mode</strong> option, choose one of the following:<ul><li><strong>Direct</strong></li><li><strong>Between Part Types</strong></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information on working with Item Flows, see the following chapters:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0d2a8234-7674-45bd-913f-a27021c8f7f3" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989769 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01011: (2026x Refresh1) Item Flow Manager dialog

- page_id: `303989769`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989769/2026x+Refresh1+Item+Flow+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:17:57.580+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

The **Item Flow Manager** dialog enables you to analyze and manage Item Flow data. You can:

- Edit existing Item Flows by adding new Conveyed Items
- Realize or hide existing Item Flows on selected relationships
- Change the Conveyed Item
- Review or delete existing Activity Pairs
- Review or update diagrams [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_dialog.png']

##### Opening the Item Flow Manager dialog

To open the **Item Flow****Manager** dialog

1. Select Association, Connector, Association,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_icon.png'] . The Item Flow Manager dialog opens.

##### Item Flow Manager buttons

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to add a new Item Flow. |
|  | Click to delete an Item Flow.The button is disabled if no Item Flow is selected in the dialog. |
|  | Toggle to either show or hide inherited Item Flows.The child element (subtype) in a generalization relationship contains inherited Item Flows from the parent element (supertype). |
|  | Toggle to either show or hide aggregated Item Flows. The parent (composite) element in a composition relationship contains aggregated Item Flows from all child elements (composite parts). |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle to either show or hide full element paths. |
|  | Select to review, preview, or update diagrams in which the realizing elements are shown. Clicking the button opens the Affected Diagrams dialog, which displays the diagrams that use the elements realizing the selected Item Flow.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see .The button is only available when a realized Item Flow is selected in the dialog. |
|  | Enter keyword(s) to filter the data displayed in the dialog.To open the menu with more filter options, click the button in the Filter box. |
|  | Click to realize all Item Flows displayed in the dialog. |
|  | Click not to realize any Item Flows displayed in the dialog. |
|  | Select to review, preview, or update diagrams in which the realizing elements are shown. Clicking the button opens the All Affected Diagrams dialog, which displays the diagrams that use the elements realizing the Item Flows displayed in the Item Flow Manager.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see .The button is only available when there is at least one realized Item Flow in the dialog. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990534 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01012: (2026x Refresh1) Items and Data

- page_id: `303990534`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990534/2026x+Refresh1+Items+and+Data
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:30.691+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485899704

1485899706

1485899706

The traditional requirement of process modeling is to be able to model the items (physical or information items) that are created, manipulated, and used during the execution of a process. This requirement is fulfilled in BPMN through various constructs: Data Objects, Item Definition, Properties, Data Inputs, Data Outputs, Messages, Input Sets, Output Sets, and Data Associations.

1485899704

**Related elements**

- Data Object
- Data Store
- Data Input and Data Output
- Data Association

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This requirement is fulfilled in BPMN through various constructs: Data Objects, Item Definition, Properties, Data Inputs, Data Outputs, Messages, Input Sets, Output Sets, and Data Associations.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8243c56e-5140-4aa7-936a-40c20d286da1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485899704</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Data+Object">Data Object</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Data+Store">Data Store</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Data+Input+and+Data+Output">Data Input and Data Output</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Data+Association">Data Association</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990100 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01013: (2026x Refresh1) Javadoc syntax

- page_id: `303990100`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990100/2026x+Refresh1+Javadoc+syntax
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.481+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Javadoc syntax tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A doc comment consists of characters between the characters /** that begin the comment and the characters */ that end it. Leading asterisks are allowed on each line and are described below. Text in a comment can continue to multiple lines.

- Leading asterisks When Javadoc Tool parses a doc comment, leading asterisk (*) characters on each line are discarded.
- First sentence The first sentence of each doc comment is a summary sentence. This sentence can be retrieved from $doc.firstSentenceTags. The first sentence tags are a collection of inline tags until full stops (.).
- Comment and tag sections A comment is the main description which begins after the starting delimiters /** and continues until the tag section. A tag section starts with the first block tag. The comment can be retrieved by $doc.comment
- Comments written in HTML Text is written in HTML and will be rendered as HTML support before being printed to a report. (See Appendix D: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Appendix D: HTML tag support' space=''] )
- **Block and in-line tags** 
A tag is a special keyword within a doc comment that Javadoc Tool can process. There are two kinds of tags: 
**(i) Block tags** 
 They can be placed only in the tag section. The block tag form is @tag. 
 The block tag can be accessed directly from the root document such as $doc.param and $doc.author. 
 The $doc.tags will provide a collection of all the tags appearing in this Javadoc. **(ii) Inline tags** 
 They can be placed anywhere in the main description or in the block tags. The inline tag form is {@tag}.

The table below lists Current Supported Tags

| Tag | JDK | Report Wizard Support |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @author | 1.0 | Yes |
| {@code} | 1.5 | Render as <code>text</code> |
| {@docRoot} | 1.3 | Not supported |
| @deprecated | 1.0 | Yes |
| @exception | 1.0 | Yes |
| {@inheritDoc} | 1.4 | Not supported |
| {@link} | 1.2 | Yes, with conditions (External link, model support, and class/method link will be ignored) |
| {@linkplain} | 1.4 | Yes, see {@link link} |
| {@literal} | 1.5 | Yes |
| @param | 1.0 | Yes |
| @return | 1.0 | Yes |
| @see | 1.0 | Yes, with conditions (External link, model support, and class/method link will be return as plain text) |
| @serial | 1.2 | Yes |
| @serialData | 1.2 | Yes |
| @serialField | 1.2 | Yes |
| @since | 1.1 | Yes |
| @throws | 1.2 | Yes |
| {@value} | 1.4 | Not Supported |
| @version | 1.0 | Yes |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A doc comment consists of characters between the characters /** that begin the comment and the characters */ that end it. Leading asterisks are allowed on each line and are described below. Text in a comment can continue to multiple lines.</p><ul><li><strong>Leading asterisks</strong><br />When Javadoc Tool parses a doc comment, leading asterisk (*) characters on each line are discarded.</li><li><strong>First sentence</strong><br />The first sentence of each doc comment is a summary sentence. <br />This sentence can be retrieved from $doc.firstSentenceTags. <br />The first sentence tags are a collection of inline tags until full stops (.).</li><li><strong>Comment and tag sections</strong><br />A comment is the main description which begins after the starting delimiters /** and <br />continues until the tag section. A tag section starts with the first block tag. The  comment can be retrieved by $doc.comment </li><li><strong>Comments written in HTML</strong><br />Text is written in HTML and will be rendered as HTML support before being printed to a report. (See Appendix D: <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Appendix D: HTML tag support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[HTML Tag Support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</li><li><p><strong>Block and in-line tags</strong><br />A tag is a special keyword within a doc comment that Javadoc Tool can process. There are two kinds of tags:<br /><strong>     (i) Block tags</strong><br />         They can be placed only in the tag section. The block tag form is @tag. <br />         The block tag can be accessed directly from the root document such as $doc.param and        $doc.author. <br />         The $doc.tags will provide a collection of all the tags appearing in this Javadoc.</p><p> <strong>    (ii) Inline tags</strong><br />          They can be placed anywhere in the main description or in the block tags. The inline tag form is {@tag}.</p></li></ul><p>The table below lists Current Supported Tags</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 38.2806%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.8092%;" /><col style="width: 9.44123%;" /><col style="width: 69.8683%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Tag</th><th>JDK</th><th>Report Wizard Support</th></tr><tr><td>@author </td><td>1.0</td><td>Yes</td></tr><tr><td>{@code}</td><td>1.5</td><td>Render as <br />&lt;code&gt;text&lt;/code&gt;</td></tr><tr><td>{@docRoot}</td><td>1.3</td><td>Not supported</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@deprecated</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@exception</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">{@inheritDoc}</td><td colspan="1">1.4</td><td colspan="1">Not supported</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">{@link}</td><td colspan="1">1.2</td><td colspan="1">Yes, with conditions <br />(External link, model support, <br />and class/method link will be <br />ignored)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">{@linkplain}</td><td colspan="1">1.4</td><td colspan="1">Yes, see <br />{@link link}</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">{@literal}</td><td colspan="1">1.5</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@param</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@return</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@see</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">Yes, with conditions <br />(External link, model <br />support, and class/method <br />link will be return as plain text)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@serial</td><td colspan="1">1.2</td><td colspan="1">Yes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@serialData</td><td colspan="1">1.2</td><td colspan="1"><span>Yes</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@serialField</td><td colspan="1">1.2</td><td colspan="1"><span>Yes</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@since</td><td colspan="1">1.1</td><td colspan="1"><span>Yes</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@throws</td><td colspan="1">1.2</td><td colspan="1"><span>Yes</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">{@value}</td><td colspan="1">1.4</td><td colspan="1">Not Supported</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">@version</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1"><span>Yes</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990097 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01014: (2026x Refresh1) Javadoc syntax tool

- page_id: `303990097`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990097/2026x+Refresh1+Javadoc+syntax+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.414+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Javadoc is the industry standard for documenting Java classes. Javadoc can be documented inside the documentation field, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Documentation Field.

Report Wizard provides a template tool for translating Javadoc text into Javadoc properties. Javadoc tool will be loaded and used inside the template upon the user’s request.

An example of Javadoc text is shown below:

```text
* Observable. The observer class such as
 * {@link AbstractTemplateEngine} or graphical user
 * interface can catch notified object and interact with
 * user.
 *
 * @author Siri Chongasamethaworn (siri_c@nomagicasia.com)
 * @since Aug 3, 2007
 */]]>
```

To print the Javadoc comment and author inside the template:

```text

```

The output from this example will be:

```text

```

The Javadoc Syntax tool is implemented based on Custom tool (See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions' space='']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Javadoc is the industry standard for documenting Java classes. Javadoc can be documented inside the documentation field, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Documentation Field.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Javadoc syntax tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Documentation Field.</h6><p>Report Wizard provides a template tool for translating Javadoc text into Javadoc properties. Javadoc tool will be loaded and used inside the template upon the user’s request.</p><p>An example of Javadoc text is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8154414e-86db-4ea7-b2f1-7de71b435528"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/**
 * A base class realizing from interface {@link Itool}.
 * This class provides default implementation for
 * {@link Itool}. The extended class from this class may
 * send message or object to observer class by invoking
 * {@link #notifyObservers(Object)} of class <code>
 * Observable</code>. The observer class such as
 * {@link AbstractTemplateEngine} or graphical user
 * interface can catch notified object and interact with
 * user.
 *
 * @author Siri Chongasamethaworn (siri_c@nomagicasia.com)
 * @since Aug 3, 2007
 */]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To print the Javadoc comment and author inside the template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="566fb076-8c75-47ad-856e-c1c6acdba6f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ("javadoc", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.doc.JavaDoctool")
#set ($doc = $javadoc.create($class.documentation))
Comment:
$doc.comment
Author:
$doc.author]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output from this example will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bffdd3df-7bb6-48da-92dc-13497c2b1103"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Comment:
A base class realizing from interface Itool. This class provides default implementation for Itool. The extended class from this class may send message or object to observer class by invoke #notifyObservers(Object) of class Observable. The observer class such as AbstractTemplateEngine or graphical user interface can catch notified object and interact with user.
Author:
Siri Chongasamethaworn (siri_c@nomagicasia.com)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Javadoc Syntax tool is implemented based on Custom tool (See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions" /></ac:link>).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990207 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01015: (2026x Refresh1) Javadoc tool API

- page_id: `303990207`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990207/2026x+Refresh1+Javadoc+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:17.205+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Javadoc syntax tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

133364031

133364033

133364028

**On this page**

43

133364032

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Javadoc Tool API.

#### JavaDocTool

JavaDoc Tool is a custom tool class used for creating a Document node.

- create() - Return a new instance of a Document node. Each JavaDoc comment must start with /** and ended with */.
- createForText() - Return a new instance of a Document node. This method allows in-complete JavaDoc (comments without /** and */) to be loaded with JavaDocTool.

##### Document

Document is an interface representing a Comment Document.

- getRawComment() - Return the full unprocessed text of the comment.
- getTags() - Return all tags in this document item.
- getTags(tagName : String) - Return a tag of the specified kind of this document item.
- get(tagName : String) - Return a tag of the specified kind of this document item. If any tags with the same kind are found, the first tag of that kind will be returned.

##### DocumentImpl

DocumentImpl is a default JavaDoc document implementation.

- getComment() - Return formatted text for this document.
- getInlineTags() - Return comment as a collection of tags.
- getFirstSentenceTags() - Return the first sentence of the comment as a collection of tags.

##### Tag

Tag is an interface representing a simple documentation tag, such as @since, @author, and @version

- getKind() - Return the kind of this tag.
- getText() - Return the text of this tag.
- getInlineTags() - Return a collection of tags for a documentation comment with embedded {@link} tags.

##### TagImpl

TagImpl is a default tag implementation.

- getRawText() - Return the full unprocessed text of this tag.
- toString() - Return the text of this tag. Usually calls getText().

##### ParamTag

ParamTag represents a @param documentation tag.

- getName() - Return the name of the parameter associated with this tag.
- getComment() - Return the parameter’s comment.

##### ThrowsTag

ThrowsTag represents a @throws or @exception documentation tag.

- getName() - Return the name of the exception associated with this tag.
- getComment() - Return the exception’s comment.

##### SeeTag

SeeTag represents a user-defined cross-reference to related documentation. The reference can be either inline with the comment using {@link} or a separate block comment using @see.

- getLabel() - Return the label of the @see tag.
- getReference() - Return the MODEL or URL of the @see reference.
- getLink() - Return the Link object representing this tag.

##### SerialFieldTag

SerialFieldTag represents a @serialField tag.

- getFieldName() - return the serial field name.
- getFieldDescription() - return the field comment.
- getFieldType() - return the field type string.

For examples:

- Import the Javadoc tool and create an instance of Javadoc document:

```text

```

- Print a Javadoc comment:

```text

```

- Print the first encountered author:

```text

```

- Print all authors:

```text

```

- Print the first sentence:

```text

```

- Print the inline tags:

```text

```

- Print a raw comment (plain text without a document parser or HTML renderer):

```text

```

- Print all block tags:

```text

```

- Print all param tags:

```text

```

- Print all throws tags:

```text

```

- Print all see tags:

```text

```

**Reference Documents**

- Javadoc specification [http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.5.0/docs/tooldocs/windows/javadoc.html#javadoctags](http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.5.0/docs/tooldocs/windows/javadoc.html#javadoctags)
- Writing Javadoc [http://java.sun.com/j2se/javadoc/writingdoccomments/index.html](http://java.sun.com/j2se/javadoc/writingdoccomments/index.html)
- Javadoc Tool API <install.dir>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/api/javadoc.zip

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8add90c0-1113-4eb5-b349-c489a69e8d23"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133364031</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e433ba0-d81d-4476-aeff-fad718a13ad3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133364033</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aaf4c12-6505-42ea-88eb-845014339e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133364028</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78597c05-046d-4116-be4a-1b9f24bc4cc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5c1c09c-7fc2-4568-b8a5-46fcb0722f39"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133364032</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Javadoc Tool API" ac:alt="Javadoc Tool API"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Javadoc Tool API.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Javadoc tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Javadoc Tool API.</h6><h2>JavaDocTool</h2><p>JavaDoc Tool is a custom tool class used for creating a Document node.</p><ul><li>create() - Return a new instance of a Document node. Each JavaDoc comment must start with /** and ended with */.</li><li>createForText() - Return a new instance of a Document node. This method allows in-complete JavaDoc (comments without /** and */) to be loaded with JavaDocTool.</li></ul><h3>Document</h3><p>Document is an interface representing a Comment Document.</p><ul><li>getRawComment() - Return the full unprocessed text of the comment.</li><li>getTags() - Return all tags in this document item.</li><li>getTags(tagName : String) - Return a tag of the specified kind of this document item.</li><li>get(tagName : String) - Return a tag of the specified kind of this document item. If any tags with the same kind are found, the first tag of that kind will be returned.</li></ul><h3>DocumentImpl</h3><p>DocumentImpl is a default JavaDoc document implementation.</p><ul><li>getComment() - Return formatted text for this document.</li><li>getInlineTags() - Return comment as a collection of tags.</li><li>getFirstSentenceTags() - Return the first sentence of the comment as a collection of tags.</li></ul><h3>Tag</h3><p>Tag is an interface representing a simple documentation tag, such as @since, @author, and @version</p><ul><li>getKind() - Return the kind of this tag.</li><li>getText() - Return the text of this tag.</li><li>getInlineTags() - Return a collection of tags for a documentation comment with embedded {@link} tags.</li></ul><h3>TagImpl</h3><p>TagImpl is a default tag implementation.</p><ul><li>getRawText() - Return the full unprocessed text of this tag.</li><li>toString() - Return the text of this tag. Usually calls getText().</li></ul><h3>ParamTag</h3><p>ParamTag represents a @param documentation tag.</p><ul><li>getName() - Return the name of the parameter associated with this tag.</li><li>getComment() - Return the parameter’s comment.</li></ul><h3>ThrowsTag</h3><p>ThrowsTag represents a @throws or @exception documentation tag.</p><ul><li>getName() - Return the name of the exception associated with this tag.</li><li>getComment() - Return the exception’s comment.</li></ul><h3>SeeTag</h3><p>SeeTag represents a user-defined cross-reference to related documentation. The reference can be either inline with the comment using {@link} or a separate block comment using @see.</p><ul><li>getLabel() - Return the label of the @see tag.</li><li>getReference() - Return the MODEL or URL of the @see reference.</li><li>getLink() - Return the Link object representing this tag.</li></ul><h3>SerialFieldTag</h3><p>SerialFieldTag represents a @serialField tag.</p><ul><li>getFieldName() - return the serial field name.</li><li>getFieldDescription() - return the field comment.</li><li>getFieldType() - return the field type string.</li></ul><p>For examples:</p><ul><li>Import the Javadoc tool and create an instance of Javadoc document:  </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8124e3b-fd42-45d0-804b-8dde32a4888e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ("javadoc",
"com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.doc.JavaDocTool")
#set ($doc = $javadoc.create($comment))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print a Javadoc comment:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6936442f-3898-4edd-91e9-f576f07493d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$doc.comment]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print the first encountered author:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e01ef07-18ee-48f9-98e0-0335d3fb177e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$doc.author]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print all authors: </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3611db9-f8e4-4909-926c-2434bb5963da"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($author in $doc.getTags("author"))
$author.text
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print the first sentence:<br /> </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dd9b6e2-d707-41f9-bde4-dce6dc072f9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.firstSentenceTags)
$tag.text
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print the inline tags:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10da322c-933d-41e9-a715-577f1516356e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.inlineTags)
$tag.kind
$tag.text
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print a raw comment (plain text without a document parser or HTML renderer):</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f08442a-03c9-4039-9ae2-1b9a56c42dcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$doc.rawComment]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print all block tags:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9932623b-0b9e-440b-91a1-2614c51a3614"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.tags)
$tag.kind : $tag.text
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print all param tags:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75cf5d6c-12c4-4773-836d-63cb97e2b325"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.getTags("param"))
$tag.name - $tag.comment
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print all throws tags:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac2fd64e-0d8c-4a10-bf08-546d5738069a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.getTags("throws"))
$tag.name - $tag.comment
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Print all see tags:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6dc40a3-0e70-40e7-aebc-0a98824f0fbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($tag in $doc.getTags("see"))
$tag.label - $tag.reference
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Reference Documents</strong></p><ul><li>Javadoc specification<br /><em><a href="http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.5.0/docs/tooldocs/windows/javadoc.html#javadoctags" style="line-height: 1.4285715;">http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.5.0/docs/tooldocs/windows/javadoc.html#javadoctags</a></em></li><li>Writing Javadoc<br /><em><a style="line-height: 1.4285715;" href="http://java.sun.com/j2se/javadoc/writingdoccomments/index.html">http://java.sun.com/j2se/javadoc/writingdoccomments/index.html</a></em></li><li>Javadoc Tool API<br /><em>&lt;install.dir&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/api/javadoc.zip</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995767 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01016: (2026x Refresh1) JavaScript tool

- page_id: `303995767`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995767/2026x+Refresh1+JavaScript+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:01.631+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1331136403

1331136405

1331136405

JavaScript tool enables report templates to evaluate or run JavaScript codes from templates and external JavaScript files, see figure below.

The general concept behind this JavaScript feature is to separate complex business logic from presentation logic. Complex logic should be executed by JavaScript, and presentation logic should be executed by Velocity code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Class Diagram: JavaScript tool.

JavaScript tool includes 3 methods:

(i) **'eval' Method** this method will evaluate JavaScript text, and then return the result. 
(ii) **'execute' Method** 
 this method will execute a JavaScript file, and then return the result. 
(iii) **'call' Method** 
 this method will call a JavaScript function, and then return the result.

Like other Custom tools, the JavaScript tool '*scripttool.jar*' must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. The same 'js.jar' of the Automaton plugin is used, so make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled and loaded. For further information about Custom tools and the installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tool' space='']*.

To import JavaScript tool to a template type, for example:

```text

```

1331136402

Complex logic should be executed by JavaScript, and presentation logic should be executed by Velocity code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Class Diagram: JavaScript Tool"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Class Diagram for JavaScript Tool.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) JavaScript tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Class Diagram: JavaScript tool.</h6><p>JavaScript tool includes 3 methods:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">(i) <strong>'eval' Method<br /></strong>     this method will evaluate JavaScript text, and then return the result.<br />(ii) <strong>'execute' Method</strong> <br />     this method will execute a JavaScript file, and then return the result.<br />(iii) <strong>'call' Method</strong> <br />      this method will call a JavaScript function, and then return the result.</p><p>Like other Custom tools, the JavaScript tool '<em>scripttool.jar</em>' must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. The same 'js.jar' of the Automaton plugin is used, so make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled and loaded. For further information about Custom tools and the installation, see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom tool" /></ac:link></em>.</p><p>To import JavaScript tool to a template type, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e197ca03-e937-44fd-b44d-b8b3ba427f5a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('js', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.script.JavaScriptTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba978c19-06e0-4041-810c-ebbf46c0c589"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1331136402</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2e0f353d-564d-4de8-8777-300288632573" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01017: (2026x Refresh1) JavaScript tool API

- page_id: `303999268`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999268/2026x+Refresh1+JavaScript+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:43.665+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) JavaScript tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### 'eval' Method

**eval(String script)**

This method will evaluate a JavaScript code from a string and return the result.

```text

```

**eval(String script, Object bindingObject)**

This method will evaluate a JavaScript code with a single binding object. The code will be evaluated from a string. The "importer" name will be used as a binding name for this object.

```text

```

**eval(String script, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)**

This method will evaluate a JavaScript code with a single binding object and specified binding name. The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.

```text

```

**eval(String script, Map bindingMap)**

This method will evaluate a JavaScript code with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for this binding name and binding object.

```text

```

#### 'execute' Method

**execute(String filename)**

This method will execute a JavaScript file. The filename parameter is a file path to the JavaScript file.

```text

```

**execute(String filename, Object bindingObject)**

This method will execute a JavaScript file with a single binding object. The filename parameter is a file path to the JavaScript file. The "importer" name will be used as the binding name for this object.

```text

```

```text

```

**execute(String filename, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)**

This method will execute a JavaScript file with a single binding object and specified binding name. The filename parameter is a file path to the JavaScript file. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.

```text

```

```text

```

**execute(String filename, Map bindingMap)**

This method will execute a JavaScript file with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The filename parameter is a file path to the JavaScript file. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for this binding name and binding object.

```text

```

```text

```

- Absolute Path : If a 'filename' is provided with an absolute path, JavaScript tool will read the JavaScript file from an absolute location such as $js.execute(' c:/mycode/readclass.js ').
- Relative Path : If a 'filename' is provided with a relative path, JavaScript tool will read the template from a relative location. This relative location starts from the current directory in which the template is located such as $js.execute('readclass.js').

#### 'call' Method

**call(String function)**

This method will provide a short and reusable method to call a JavaScript function. This function must be defined before calling this method. This function can be defined by 'eval' or 'execute'.

```text

```

```text

```

**call(String function, Object bindingObject)**

This method will provide a short and reusable method to call a JavaScript function with a single binding object. The "importer" name will be used as the binding name for this object. This function must be defined before calling this method. This function can be defined by 'eval' or 'execute'.

```text

```

```text

```

**call(String function, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)**

This method will provide a short and reusable method to call a JavaScript function with a single binding object and specified binding name. This binding name will be used as the name for this object. This function must be defined before calling this method. This function can be defined by 'eval' or 'execute'.

```text

```

```text

```

**call(String function, Map bindingMap)**

This method will provide a short and reusable method to call a JavaScript function with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The binding map consists of key-value pairs for this binding name and binding object.

```text

```

```text

```

## PAGE 01018: (2026x Refresh1) Key

- page_id: `303999543`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999543/2026x+Refresh1+Key
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:05.824+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute added into some UML Class.

- name – to Attribute name.
- id – to TaggedValue.

#### PANEL: key mapping to UML attributes

key mapping to UML attributes

```text
<key
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
name = NCName
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace …}>	Content: (annotation?, (selector, field+))
```

```text
</key>
```

For a key UML model example, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Keyref' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998622 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01019: (2026x Refresh1) Key modeling

- page_id: `303998622`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998622/2026x+Refresh1+Key+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:48.749+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Entity keys are marked by applying the corresponding stereotype («PrimaryKey», «AlternativeKey») on the necessary column(s). This can be done from the column's shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of key usage in an ER diagram.

Primary key columns are grouped into a separate compartment. When the «PrimaryKey» stereotype is applied / unapplied, the column migrates between the two compartments.

In rare cases, you may need to specify several alternative keys on the same entry. In this case, you will fill the “Id” tag field of the key column with key identifier(s). Columns with the same Id are considered to belong to the same key. Overlapping alternative keys can be specified in the same manner (a column can have several ids specified).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of multiple overlapping alternative keys in ER diagram.

Inversion entries are specified analogously. An inversion entry is a non-unique (combination of) column(s), frequently used to search for the entity. Marking columns as IE provides hints to database implementers about which indexes to specify.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of inversion entry in ER diagram.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Although an ER profile carries the «ForeignKey» stereotype, this stereotype is currently unused. It is reserved for future automatic foreign key derivation functionality. Users should not specify FK columns explicitly on their entities (FKs are implied), unless they are needed for a specific purpose. **Use at your own risk.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Entity keys are marked by applying the corresponding stereotype («PrimaryKey», «AlternativeKey») on the necessary column(s). This can be done from the column's shortcut menu.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 10 Key Usage in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of key usage in an ER diagram.</h6><p><br /></p><p>Primary key columns are grouped into a separate compartment. When the «PrimaryKey» stereotype is applied / unapplied, the column migrates between the two compartments.</p><p>In rare cases, you may need to specify several alternative keys on the same entry. In this case, you will fill the “Id” tag field of the key column with key identifier(s). Columns with the same Id are considered to belong to the same key. Overlapping alternative keys can be specified in the same manner (a column can have several ids specified).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 11 Multiple Overlapping Alternative Keys in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of multiple overlapping alternative keys in ER diagram.</h6><p><br /></p><p>Inversion entries are specified analogously. An inversion entry is a non-unique (combination of) column(s), frequently used to search for the entity. Marking columns as IE provides hints to database implementers about which indexes to specify.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 12 Inversion Entry in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of inversion entry in ER diagram.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67f2233a-7aab-41d3-9e3e-d75cb289e155"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Although an ER profile carries the «ForeignKey» stereotype, this stereotype is currently unused. It is reserved for future automatic foreign key derivation functionality. Users should not specify FK columns explicitly on their entities (FKs are implied), unless they are needed for a specific purpose. <strong>Use at your own risk.</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999445 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01020: (2026x Refresh1) Key transformation

- page_id: `303999445`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999445/2026x+Refresh1+Key+transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:57.474+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Transformations > (2026x Refresh1) ER to SQL (generic / Oracle) transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Keys in ER models are transformed into constraints in a DDL model. These are the rules for key transformations into DDL constraints as follows.

1. The Primary key of the entity in the ER model is transformed into a primary key constraint in the SQL model.
2. The Alternative keys of the entities in the ER model are transformed into unique constraints in the SQL model.
3. The Inversion entries of the entities in the ER model are transformed into indexes in the SQL model.
4. If key or entry in ER model has a name ( identifier tag), this information is preserved in the SQL model. The corresponding key / index will also have a name in the SQL model.

Let's review an example of key modeling, which has been described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Key modeling' space='']. After the transformation, the three entities of the ER model are transformed into the three tables of the SQL model respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TBD screenshot example of key transformation results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Keys in ER models are transformed into constraints in a DDL model. These are the rules for key transformations into DDL constraints as follows.</p><ol><li>The Primary key of the entity in the ER model is transformed into a primary key constraint in the SQL model.</li><li>The Alternative keys of the entities in the ER model are transformed into unique constraints in the SQL model.</li><li>The Inversion entries of the entities in the ER model are transformed into indexes in the SQL model.</li><li>If key or entry in ER model has a name (<strong>identifier </strong>tag), this information is preserved in the SQL model. The corresponding key / index will also have a name in the SQL model.</li></ol><p>Let's review an example of key modeling, which has been described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key modeling" /></ac:link>. After the transformation, the three entities of the ER model are transformed into the three tables of the SQL model respectively.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 41 TBD Screenshot Example of Key Transformation Results.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key transformation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">TBD screenshot example of key transformation results.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992736 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01022: (2026x Refresh1) Known Limitations and Constraints

- page_id: `303992736`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992736/2026x+Refresh1+Known+Limitations+and+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:13.106+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x Refresh1) Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following tableshows the conversion limitations and constraints in EAexported XMIand EAImport.

| No. | Constraint name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | N-ary Association | Due to the difference between the EA exported XMI and MagicDraw, CEA or CSM XMI, the view of will not be imported. |
| 2 | Diagram Legend | MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not have a similar element. Diagram legends will not be imported. |
| 3 | Diagram Note | The element most similar to Diagram Note is the MagicDraw, CEA and CSM diagram information table (Option > Show diagram info). However, in EA it will be mapped to the Option > Show diagram details. So Diagram Notes will not be imported to MagicDraw, CEA or CSM. |
| 4 | Text size | The size of text displayed in EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM may vary. The text size in EA is mostly larger. |
| 5 | Word wrap | Due to size constraints in text displayed in element blocks, the result of word wrap in EA and MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM is likely to be different. |
| 6 | Contact point between element and link | The position of both ends of a link element cannot be mapped to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM because the last segments of the link element in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM always point to the center of connected elements. |
| 7 | Display alternative image | In EA, you can display an element in a diagram by using an alternative image. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not import that particular image and will use a default shape instead. |
| 8 | Link label position | A Link element, such as an Association, has many labels, including multiplicity and role name labels. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not import the position of these labels and will use a default position instead. |
| 9 | Pin position | A position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM may have been slightly moved from its original position in EA. If the pin is placed at the corner of its containing element, it will be shifted a little away from the corner (mostly in a clockwise direction). |
| 10 | Nested CallBehavior- Action | A element nested with another CallBehaviorAction element. The outermost part of the element will remain. The other will be removed. |
| 11 | DataStoreNode inner element | A DataStoreNode element containing Activity Diagram related elements. Every element inside that particular DataStoreNode element will be removed. Only the DataStoreNode element will remain. |
| 12 | Object inner element | An Object element containing Activity Diagram related elements. Every element inside that particular Object element will be removed. Only the Object element will remain. |
| 13 | Lifeline position | The position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not correspond to the original position in EA. The position is fixed. |
| 14 | Diagonal Sequence Message | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not support Diagonal Sequence Messages. |
| 15 | Sequence Activation Options | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not support manipulating Sequence Activation through Sequence Activation Options. |
| 16 | State contains diagram | A diagram element placed inside a element will be removed. |
| 17 | Region in Orthogonal State | EA exported XMI contains incorrect information when:more than one region has identical names.a region was created and then removed before exporting to XMI.This will cause an unexpected result after importing it into MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 18 | Assembly Relationship in Composite Structure diagram | Assembly relationships in EA are exported to XMI as Connectors. They will then be imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM as . |
| 19 | Interaction elements as diagram frame | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not import Interaction elements displayed as a diagram frame in an diagram. |
| 20 | Message timing details | Duration Observation, and Timing Observation are not imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 21 | Concurrent State Regions in StateMachine | Adding and removing multiple Concurrent State Regions to and from StateMachine in EA can cause the EA XMI to be incorrectly exported. If the EA exported XMI is in this state, the result of the imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM cannot be determined.The same problem also occurs if there are multiple Concurrent State Regions with the same name. |
| 22 | Combined Fragment | Adding and removing multiple Interaction Operands to and from Combined Fragment in EA can cause the EA XMI to be incorrectly exported. The problem can be fixed by importing the EA XMI back to a new project in EA and exporting it back before importing it to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 23 | Problem occurs during conversion of . | Importing EA XMI to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM sometimes results in the following error message: “Problem occurred during conversion of Sequence diagram”. If this occurs, please try to import the XMI back to a new project in EA and export it back again before importing it to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If the pin is placed at the corner of its containing element, it will be shifted a little away from the corner (mostly in a clockwise direction).</p></td></tr><tr><td>10</td><td><span style="color: black;">Nested CallBehavior- </span><span style="color: black;">Action</span></td><td><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Call Behavior Action" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CallBehaviorAction ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element nested with another CallBehaviorAction element. The outermost part of the element will remain. The other will be removed.</p></td></tr><tr><td>11</td><td><span style="color: black;">DataStoreNode inner </span><span style="color: black;">element</span></td><td><p>A DataStoreNode element containing Activity Diagram related elements. Every element inside that particular DataStoreNode element will be removed. Only the DataStoreNode element will remain.</p></td></tr><tr><td>12</td><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Object inner element</span></td><td><span style="color: black;">An<span> </span>Object<span> </span>element<span> </span>containing<span> </span>Activity Diagram related elements.<span> </span>Every </span><span style="color: black;">element inside that<span> </span>particular Object element will be removed. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989983 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01023: (2026x Refresh1) L1 Node Types

- page_id: `303989983`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989983/2026x+Refresh1+L1+Node+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:52.406+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l1-node-types', 'l1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

62232222

62232224

62232223

**Description**

The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

The L1 view may be used to:

- Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.
- Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.
- Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.

**Implementation**

To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- L1 Node Types diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 Free FormNode Types diagram.

62232221

The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.</p><p>The L1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.</li><li>Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.</li><li>Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">L1 Node Types diagram.</span><span class="confluence-link"><br /><ac:image ac:title="L1 Node Types" ac:alt="L1 Node Types"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L1_node_types.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) L1 Node Types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram.</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989997 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01024: (2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario

- page_id: `303989997`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989997/2026x+Refresh1+L2+Logical+Internal+Scenario
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:56.496+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l2-logical-scenario', 'l2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

63480270

63480272

63480271

**Description**

The L2i Logical Internal Scenario view shows, at a high level, the interactions between logical nodes and places them in the context of location(s). The primary purpose of the L2i view is to specify nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario). The context may also be flows of material, human resource or energy.

The L2i view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.

**Implementation**

The L2i view can be represented using an L2i diagram which is based on theUML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

63480269

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Known Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

## PAGE 01025: (2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Scenario

- page_id: `303989960`

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L2 Logical Scenario view identifies the operational exchange requirements between nodes. It defines operational architecture and exchange requirements necessary to support a specific set of Capabilities.

**Implementation**

The L2 view canberepresented using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the L2 Logical Scenario diagram.
