<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994013 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01026: (2026x Refresh1) L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy

- page_id: `303994013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994013/2026x+Refresh1+L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:49.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687974

1977687976

1977687975

**Description**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.

**Implementation**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram' space=''] . The primary purpose of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. The intended usage of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - Definition of operational concepts.
  - Elaboration of capability requirements.
  - Definition of collaboration needs.
  - Applying a local context to a capability.
  - Problem space definition.
  - Operational planning.
  - Supply chain analysis.
  - Allocation of activities to resources.

1977687973

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995390 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01027: (2026x Refresh1) L3 Node Interactions

- page_id: `303995390`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995390/2026x+Refresh1+L3+Node+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:30.325+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l3-node-interactions', 'l3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64204026

64204029

64204027

**Description**

The L3 Node Interactions view addresses operational information exchanges between nodes. Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.

Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

The L3 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define Problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Analyze Supply chain.
- Secure models – e.g. domain-based security & entity trust models.

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- L3 Node Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3.PNG']

- L3 Node Role Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3_node_role_interactions.png']

- L3 spreadsheet report.

64204025

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995384 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01028: (2026x Refresh1) L4 Logical Activities

- page_id: `303995384`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995384/2026x+Refresh1+L4+Logical+Activities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:29.832+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l4-logical-activities', 'l4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64503150

64503152

64503151

**Description**

The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space=''] are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions (P4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.
- Information exchanged between business processes.
- External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).

The L4 view may be used to: 
 • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='']. 
 • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy. 
 • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities. 
 • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further. 
 • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L8 Logical Constraints' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L5 Logical States' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L6 Logical Sequence' space=''].

**Implementation**

The L4 view can be represented using:

- L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4_flow_model.png']

- L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4.png']
- L4 Logical Activities (BPD) diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

64503149

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Parameter' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998845 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01029: (2026x Refresh1) L5 Logical States

- page_id: `303998845`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998845/2026x+Refresh1+L5+Logical+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:14.119+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l5-logical-states', 'l5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L4 Logical Activities' space=''].

**Implementation**

The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995405 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01030: (2026x Refresh1) L6 Logical Sequence

- page_id: `303995405`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995405/2026x+Refresh1+L6+Logical+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:31.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l6-logical-sequence', 'l6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L6 Logical Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating Operational Nodes as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='']. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L5 Logical States' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.

Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.

The L6 view may be used to:

- Analyze operational events.
- Sequences of interactions between nodes.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995400 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01031: (2026x Refresh1) L7 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `303995400`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995400/2026x+Refresh1+L7+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:31.375+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l7-logical-data-model', 'l7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65804908

65804910

65804909

**Description**

The L7 Logical Data Model view addresses the information perspective on an operational architecture. The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The L7 view may be used for:

- Information architecture.
- Information product hierarchy.

**Implementation**

The L7 view can be represented using:

- L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L7.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

65804907

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Association' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

****

The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The L7 view may be used for:</p><ul><li>Information architecture.</li><li>Information product hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L7 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="L7 Logical Data Model diagram" ac:alt="L7 Logical Data Model diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) L7 Logical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10edf244-13a3-4847-ab2b-09818f2475aa">UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10edf244-13a3-4847-ab2b-09818f2475aa">SysML Block Definition diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e83164-922d-4fc8-bf57-96fddc37cf0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65804907</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a677bbbb-3715-4e3d-a155-47c48b700e15" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995410 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01032: (2026x Refresh1) L8 Logical Constraints

- page_id: `303995410`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995410/2026x+Refresh1+L8+Logical+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:32.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l8-logical-constraints', 'l8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

66061943

66061946

66061944

**Description**

The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.

This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.

The L8 view may be used to:

- Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Define business rules.
- Identify operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The L8 view can be represented using:

- L8 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L8.PNG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L8 Logical Parametric' space=''] diagram.
- L8 spreadsheet report.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

66061942

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

## PAGE 01033: (2026x Refresh1) L8 Logical Parametric

- page_id: `303995413`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:32.791+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498290670

498290672

498290671

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498290669

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating L8 table' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994590 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01034: (2026x Refresh1) Label layout in the diagram

- page_id: `303994590`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994590/2026x+Refresh1+Label+layout+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:26.736+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Layout
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Default label positions

Default label positions are retained after moving a path, shape, or related element if it is a semantically logical decision. See the example below, where association multiplicities remain at their default positions after class is moved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If labels are at their default position, reset labels position functionality is not available.

##### Label positions after moving a path, shape, or related element

After moving a path, shape, or related element, default label positions are retained if it is a semantically logical decision.

For a nicer representation of labels in a diagram, the label positions are reset to default automatically if:

1. Symbol properties are edited. When symbol properties are edited by adding or removing a label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
2. Path, path end, or port properties are edited. When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
3. Path, shape, or related element are moved.

To check the label deviation from its default position

- Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. This helps to show the current label's owner.

When the label is not in its default position, you may indicate it by the icon on the bottom right corner, which appears after selecting the label's owner.

##### Label consideration mode

When arranging shapes for the hierarchic layout mechanisms, you may choose the **Label Consideration** mode:

- Hierarchic Layout.
- Class Diagram Layout.
- Composite Diagram Layout.
- Activity Diagram Layout.
- State Machine Diagram Layout.
- Business Process Diagram Layout.
- Ordered Hierarchic Layout.

In order to do this, you must specify the **Label consideration** mode (Full, Partial, or None) in the **Diagram Layout Options** dialog.

To specify the label consideration mode

1. From the diagram toolbar, expand [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.png'] and select Layout Options command.
2. On the left side of the dialog, select one of the following layout styles: - Under the General Layout group, select Hierarchic Layout . - Under the Specialized Layout group, select any layout style:
  - Class Diagram Layout.
  - Composite Diagram Layout.
  - Activity Diagram Layout.
  - State Machine Diagram Layout.
  - Business Process Diagram Layout.
  - Ordered Hierarchic Layout.
3. On the right side of the dialog, find the Label consideration option.
4. Click on the value of this option and select one of the following modes:
  - None - does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.
  - Partial - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.
  - Full - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.
5. Click the Layout button.
6. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.</li><li>Path, shape, or related element are moved.</li></ol><p>To check the label deviation from its default position</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. This helps to show the current label's owner.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4dbb75f-65ca-4033-a7c5-0ed2722274c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the label is not in its default position, you may indicate it by the icon on the bottom right corner, which appears after selecting the label's owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Label consideration mode</h3><p>When arranging shapes for the hierarchic layout mechanisms, you may choose the <strong>Label Consideration</strong> mode:</p><ul><li>Hierarchic Layout.</li><li>Class Diagram Layout.</li><li>Composite Diagram Layout.</li><li>Activity Diagram Layout.</li><li>State Machine Diagram Layout.</li><li>Business Process Diagram Layout.</li><li>Ordered Hierarchic Layout.</li></ul><p>In order to do this, you must specify the <strong>Label consideration</strong> mode (Full, Partial, or None) in the <strong>Diagram Layout Options</strong> dialog.</p><p>To specify the label consideration mode</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram toolbar, expand <ac:image ac:title="Quick Diagram Layout" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Quick Diagram Layout" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_diagram_layout.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Options</strong> command.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, select one of the following layout styles:<br />  - Under the <strong>General Layout</strong> group, select <strong>Hierarchic Layout</strong>.<br />  - Under the <strong>Specialized Layout</strong> group, select any layout style:<ul><li>Class Diagram Layout.</li><li>Composite Diagram Layout.</li><li>Activity Diagram Layout.</li><li>State Machine Diagram Layout.</li><li>Business Process Diagram Layout.</li><li>Ordered Hierarchic Layout.</li></ul></li><li>On the right side of the dialog, find the <strong>Label consideration</strong> option.</li><li>Click on the value of this option and select one of the following modes:<br /><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>None</strong> - does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.</li><li><strong>Partial</strong> - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.</li><li><strong>Full</strong> - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Layout</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994709 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01035: (2026x Refresh1) Label orientation

- page_id: `303994709`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994709/2026x+Refresh1+Label+orientation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:41.306+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Layout
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097048

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097051

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097050

You can optimize diagram layout by changing the orientation of symbol labels. Rotate labels horizontally or vertically, or select the automatic label orientation option, which adjusts labels according to the position of shapes or paths.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This sample diagrams illustrate how changing label orientation can optimize diagram layout.

To change the orientation of independent labels

1. In an open diagram, select one or several labels that you want to rotate.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of shapes or paths they are on.

To change the orientation of labels on a shape or path

1. In an open diagram, select the path or shape on which you want to rotate labels.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels on the shape or path are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of the shape or path.

#### NOTE: Label Orientation symbol property

Label Orientation symbol property

When you change the orientation of individual labels or labels displayed on a shape or path, the value of the **Label Orientation** symbol property of the related shapes or paths changes as well. To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog, right-click a symbol in a diagram and select **Symbol Properties**.

##### Default label orientation

By default, all labels on shapes and paths are displayed horizontally. You can change the default label orientation for a project as explained below.

To change the default label orientation

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Shapes project option group.
3. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created shapes horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created shapes vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created shapes according to their position in a diagram.
4. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Paths project option group.
5. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created paths horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created paths vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created paths according to their position in a diagram.
6. Click OK on the bottom of the Project Options dialog.

The labels of newly created symbols in the diagrams of a project will be displayed according to your selected property values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: <strong>Horizontal</strong>, <strong>Vertical</strong>, or <strong>Automatic</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br />The labels are rotated to the selected position. If you select the <strong>Automatic</strong> label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of shapes or paths they are on.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the orientation of labels on a shape or path</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In an open diagram, select the path or shape on which you want to rotate labels.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:<ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rotate_label.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Label orientation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rotate_label.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Label orientation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: <strong>Horizontal</strong>, <strong>Vertical</strong>, or <strong>Automatic</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br />The labels on the shape or path are rotated to the selected position. If you select the <strong>Automatic</strong> label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of the shape or path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="426039ba-c28d-4122-8789-b212474166a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Label Orientation symbol property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you change the orientation of individual labels or labels displayed on a shape or path, the value of the <strong>Label Orientation</strong> symbol property of the related shapes or paths changes as well. To open the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, right-click a symbol in a diagram and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Default label orientation</h3><p>By default, all labels on shapes and paths are displayed horizontally. You can change the default label orientation for a project as explained below.</p><p>To change the default label orientation</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>Symbol styles </strong>&gt; <strong>Default </strong>&gt; <strong>Shapes</strong> project option group.</li><li>On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the <strong>Label Orientation</strong> property and select one of the following values:<ul><li><strong>Horizontal</strong> - to display the labels of newly created shapes horizontally.</li><li><strong>Vertical</strong> - to display the labels of newly created shapes vertically.</li><li><strong>Automatic</strong> - to display the labels of newly created shapes according to their position in a diagram.</li></ul></li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>Symbol styles </strong>&gt; <strong>Default </strong>&gt; <strong>Paths</strong> project option group.</li><li>On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the <strong>Label Orientation</strong> property and select one of the following values:<ul><li><strong>Horizontal</strong> - to display the labels of newly created paths horizontally.</li><li><strong>Vertical</strong> - to display the labels of newly created paths vertically.</li><li><strong>Automatic</strong> - to display the labels of newly created paths according to their position in a diagram.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> on the bottom of the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p><br />The labels of newly created symbols in the diagrams of a project will be displayed according to your selected property values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000658 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01036: (2026x Refresh1) Launchers

- page_id: `304000658`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000658/2026x+Refresh1+Launchers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:31.504+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Launchers show which programs will be used for opening different file types in the application. Add new, edit, or remove launchers and specify their properties (file extension, program path or name, program arguments).

To open the **Launcher Properties** dialog

1. From the **Options** menu, choose **Environment**.
2. In the Environment Options dialog, from the options group list select **Launchers**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify properties of the default launchers

1. To edit the Default launcher, click the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**button next to the **Default Launcher** or**Default Browser** text boxes.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. You can change Launcher`s executable file and arguments.

To create the defined launcher

1. Click the Add button, found below the Defined Launchers list.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. Specify the file extension, which will be opened with the specified launcher.
4. Type the name of the launcher in the text box, or browse to the location of the launcher by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Specify external launcher arguments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Properties are described in the following table.

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Extensions | Type the extensions of the files which will be opened with the specified launcher. You can define more than one extension. Separate them using commas (i.e., html, xls, doc, cpp, h). |
| Launcher | An executable file with which the extension file will be opened. |
| Arguments | Arguments are required by the external launcher, so the modeling tool provides the ability to pass some common arguments to the launcher:$f - a file name$md_dir - the modeling tool installation directory$prj_dir - the project directory (if the project is saved)$r - a row (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action)$c - a column (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action). |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994587 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01037: (2026x Refresh1) Layout

- page_id: `303994587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994587/2026x+Refresh1+Layout
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:26.305+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903811718

903811721

903811720

The diagram layout engine is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

It is easy to manage simple or complex diagrams using automated layout features that optimize diagram layout.

Symbols are automatically laid out on diagrams when displaying related elements, creating diagrams by using wizards, and creating related diagrams.

You can layout diagrams quickly by using the Quick Diagram layout. The quick layout is a recommended type of layout, and it is specific for each diagram.

The layout mechanism is built on various layout tools. There are 2 groups of layout tools: general layout tools and specific diagram layout tools. These are the general layout tools:

- Orthogonal Layout
- Hierarchic Layout
- Organic Layout
- Tree Layout
- Circular Layout
- Oblique Path Router
- Rectilinear Path Router

These layout tools are provided by yFiles layout tool component. You can arrange each diagram (except the Sequence diagram) by using any of the following general layout tools:

- Class Diagram Layout
- Composite Structure Diagram Layout
- Activity Diagram Layout
- State Machine Diagram Layout
- Business Process Diagram Layout

To layout diagrams by using the quick diagram layout, do one of the following

- Press Ctrl+Q.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button.
- On the Main menu, click Layout > Quick Diagram Layout .

To layout diagrams by using layout tools, do one of the following

- On the Main menu, click Layout and select one of the layout tools.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the small arrow next to the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button, and select one of the layout tools.
- Select the element symbols you want to layout, right-click any of them, and in the shortcut menu, select Layout , then select one of the layout options.

To change the diagram layout options

1. On the Main menu, click Layout > Layout Options .
2. In the Diagram Layout Options dialog, specify the layout options.
3. Click OK .

903811717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996523 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01038: (2026x Refresh1) Layout template creation mode

- page_id: `303996523`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996523/2026x+Refresh1+Layout+template+creation+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:07.103+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Layout templates > (2026x Refresh1) Creating layout template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.

To specify layout template creation mode

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the General node and select the General option group.
3. Click the specification cell of the Layout Template Creation Mode option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:
  - Usage - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.
  - Definition - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying layout template creation mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify layout template creation mode</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select the <strong>General</strong> option group.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Layout Template Creation Mode</strong> option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:<br /><ul><li><strong>Usage</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.</li><li><strong>Definition</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_creation_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Layout template creation mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specifying layout template creation mode.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990969 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01039: (2026x Refresh1) Layout templates

- page_id: `303990969`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990969/2026x+Refresh1+Layout+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:56.244+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The layout templates functionality, which is driven by <<layoutTemplate>> stereotype, assists you in creating well-formed diagrams. It allows you to define Class appearance once, and reuse it later when creating Parts typed by that Class and Ports in different diagrams. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] and their Ports are represented identically as part's type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] whose layout and style is defined in the layout template diagram. Often, Port position reflects its physical location in the system, so it is beneficial to maintain the same appearance of Parts, layout, and style of Ports throughout the whole model. In the figure below, the appearance of the *Modem Card* Block is the same in two different diagrams, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

You can reuse layout templates in other projects by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Usage of a layout template. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

You can create a layout template in the Class Diagram or derive it from an existing Composite Structure Diagram as described below.

To define a layout template in the Class Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining layout template' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting diagram as layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template' space='']

To derive a layout template for the Composite Structure Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Layout template creation mode' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template' space='']

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

Read the following pages to learn how to manage layout templates:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Stopping to use diagram as layout template' space='']
- Removing applied layout template from shape
- Multiple layout template
- Setting layout template as default
- Navigation to layout templates
- Using layout templates with diagram aspects

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram aspects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The layout templates functionality, which is driven by &lt;&lt;layoutTemplate&gt;&gt; stereotype, assists you in creating well-formed diagrams. It allows you to define Class appearance once, and reuse it later when creating Parts typed by that Class and Ports in different diagrams. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and their Ports are represented identically as part's type <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> whose layout and style is defined in the layout template diagram. Often, Port position reflects its physical location in the system, so it is beneficial to maintain the same appearance of Parts, layout, and style of Ports throughout the whole model. In the figure below, the appearance of the <em>Modem Card</em> Block is the same in two different diagrams, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Block Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7be13953-1f28-42da-ae50-f4aeb153dd51"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can reuse layout templates in other projects by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting packages to new projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exporting packages to new projects.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="block_layout_templates.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Layout templates" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Usage of a layout template. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6><p>You can create a layout template in the Class Diagram or derive it from an existing Composite Structure Diagram as described below.</p><p><br />To define a layout template in the Class Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:</p><ol><li data-uuid="3431e1ad-2a1f-421d-b8fe-fce24c49689f"><p><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Defining layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Define the appearance of Class, layout, and style of its Ports in the Class diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></li><li data-uuid="bbe53ce6-62ba-4d8a-a0da-2a461172ecad"><p><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting diagram as layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set that Class diagram as the layout template.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></li><li data-uuid="d149d638-f2cd-4d8a-a6db-06527c43bf6b"><p><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Apply the layout template to other diagrams.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></li></ol><p class="confluence-link">To derive a layout template for the Composite Structure Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:</p><ol><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="5ba1fdc9-b2a8-4bd6-aecc-623e7b11da1e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Layout template creation mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams or Composite Structure Diagrams.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="5e19eeb3-e4b2-4d5c-a99a-0dc67b69aebe"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a layout template from a Part Property in the Composite Structure Diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="4a18a043-6e21-4271-81e0-b5001a5488c0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Apply the layout template to other diagrams.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8579265-ff01-4631-a7bb-2ec607d5bbd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Read the following pages to learn how to manage layout templates:</p><ul><li data-uuid="07dbbbcb-4d74-4ec7-b180-85a859ea0706"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Stopping to use diagram as layout template" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="05b37cb2-69f2-4f4d-b427-a0c310e57234"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Removing+applied+layout+template+from+shape">Removing applied layout template from shape</a></li><li data-uuid="cf8e3313-257f-454e-b84e-3b944bcd9bdb"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Multiple+layout+template">Multiple layout template</a></li><li data-uuid="b5c3bae9-b59f-4a95-b8f8-ee21f090f6a5"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Setting+layout+template+as+default">Setting layout template as default</a></li><li data-uuid="90617197-af13-420c-a6c7-93aca1c72f95"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Navigation+to+layout+templates">Navigation to layout templates</a></li><li data-uuid="8d5137ba-2f6a-4c28-84da-ba45c9ef409e"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Using+layout+templates+with+diagram+aspects">Using layout templates with diagram aspects</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="28d157f4-d985-486b-96ed-d86280a179a2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="186a50fa-e3c9-4dd5-a61b-2120c8ac65a4"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bc118e33-94a1-4c65-9d62-e28d9d466184"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagram aspects" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="15e37a14-9e1e-429d-a9be-df75780d865d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting packages to new projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01041: (2026x Refresh1) Legends

- page_id: `303993088`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993088/2026x+Refresh1+Legends
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:47.000+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tips for working with Legends

37096963

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tips for working with Legends

37096965

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tips for working with Legends

37096964

A Legend is a model element that allows you to define the styles of diagram symbols and table rows. It helps you to visually group model elements according to specified criteria. Use Legends to increase the readability of the diagram and table contents by highlighting important information, e.g., specific element property values or statuses. In addition, the styles applied to model elements are dynamically updated when you modify your model, so all changes are instantly visible in diagrams and tables where Legends are used.

800450

#### TIP: Tips for working with Legends

Tips for working with Legends

- You can apply multiple Legend Items to a single model element.
- You can reuse Legends in different diagrams and tables of a model, as well as in different models.

For more information about working with Legends, see the following pages:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Risk* and *Status* legends used in a Requirement Diagram.

37096962

ac:macro-id="0eb16838-a6ae-4c55-8088-f4073f967f64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tips for working with Legends</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can apply multiple Legend Items to a single model element.</li><li>You can reuse Legends in different diagrams and tables of a model, as well as in different models.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>For more information about working with Legends, see the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2ad2d65f-afac-4afc-9997-f7634a2065de" /></p><p><br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990672 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01042: (2026x Refresh1) Length

- page_id: `303990672`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990672/2026x+Refresh1+Length
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:51.774+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDlength. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: Length XML representation summary

Length XML representation summary

```text
<length
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</length>
```

#### PANEL: Length XML code sample

Length XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype to represent product codes which must be exactly 8 characters in length. By fixing the value of the length facet, we ensure that the type derived from productCode can change or set the values of other facets, such as pattern, but cannot change the length.
```

```text
<simpleType name= 'productCode' >
```

```text
<restriction base='string'>
```

```text
<length value='8' fixed='true'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDlength. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;">value – to Attribute initial value.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e7c356e-473c-4395-bd83-6ba97779be72"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Length XML representation summary</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;length</pre><pre>	fixed = boolean : false</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	value = nonNegativeInteger</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/length&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cda1f73-6de9-46b6-9a92-2b5e35c34640"><ac:parameter ac:name="title"> Length XML code sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype to represent product codes which must be exactly 8 characters in length. <br />By fixing the value of the length facet, we ensure that the type derived from productCode can change or set the values of other facets, <br />such as pattern, but cannot change the length.<br /><br /></pre><pre>&lt;simpleType name= 'productCode' &gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;restriction base='string'&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;length value='8' fixed='true'/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/restriction&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/simpleType&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997955 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01044: (2026x Refresh1) Limiting image size

- page_id: `303997955`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997955/2026x+Refresh1+Limiting+image+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:49.164+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools > (2026x Refresh1) Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.

**$image.limitImageSize(image)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

**$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)**

Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidth** and **maximumHeight** in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidth | java.lang.String | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
|  | maximumHeight | java.lang.String | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

******$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidthPixel** and **maximumHeightPixel** in terms of pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidthPixel | int | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
|  | maximumHeightPixel | int | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.</p><p><br /> <strong>$image.limitImageSize(image)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6b15a92-9c83-4056-8168-78192a71a472"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)</strong></p><p>Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidth</strong> and <strong>maximumHeight</strong> in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidth</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeight</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e69527f-1cbb-461f-80ec-d23fb3f8884d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "-1", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "-1")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5378cabf-6a1d-4811-b8d1-bcbbe5cc3e04"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidthPixel</strong> and <strong>maximumHeightPixel</strong> in terms of pixels.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidthPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeightPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db4c789-1956-4292-8f14-d5c9fe867096"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, -1, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, -1)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="043cfae2-7bd4-41f7-a75f-d84671a615a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998854 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01045: (2026x Refresh1) Link Catching Intermediate Event

- page_id: `303998854`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998854/2026x+Refresh1+Link+Catching+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:29.541+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Intermediate Catch Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Link Catching Intermediate Event provides the capability to connect two sections of a process. You can use this event to either:

- Create looping situations or to avoid long sequence flow lines, as “Off-Page Connectors” to print a Process across multiple pages, or as generic Go To objects within a Process level.
- Catch a link from a Link Throwing Intermediate Event.

You can only use one Link Event for each single Process level, meaning that it cannot link a parent Process with a SubProcess.

A Link Catching Intermediate Event displays either of the following:

- A Link Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Source property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

****[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Link Catching Intermediate Event

**Related element**

- Intermediate Catch Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']
